Japanese: ‥‥はあ‥‥　はあ‥‥
‥‥くそっ！ なんでぼくがこんな目に‥‥
English: Grr!! How did I get into this mess...?

Japanese: ‥‥そこまでだ！‥‥
English: That's far enough!

Japanese: ‥‥もう、逃がしませぬぞ‥‥ 成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: You can't run forever, Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: い‥‥いったい、 ぼくが何をしたと言うんですか！
English: Wha...!? What have I done wrong!?

Japanese: ‥‥あなたを生かして おくわけには行かない‥‥
English: I cannot allow you to go on like this!

Japanese: で、でもぼくは、 ただのしがない弁護士ですよ‥‥？
English: B-But I'm just a simple defense attorney!

Japanese: 問答無用ッ！
English: Silence!

Japanese: ‥あなたを弁護士とは認めません‥
English: You are no longer worthy of your title.

Japanese: ‥‥いつかどこかで見たユメ‥‥
English: ...I've had this dream before; someplace, sometime ago...

Japanese: ‥‥この日のことを 予言していたのだろうか‥‥
English: ...As if this day was written into my destiny...

Japanese: ‥‥今日。ぼくは 殺人者の無実を立証する‥‥
English: ...Today I'll stand in court as a lawyer...

Japanese: ‥‥“弁護士”として‥‥
English: ...To prove a killer innocent.

Japanese: ３月２３日　午前　９時４３分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: March 23, 9:43 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: はい。成歩堂です。
English: Hello, this is Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥元気がありませんねえ。
English: You don't look so well, dude.

Japanese: 今日こそ、ボクを 無罪にしてくれるんですよね？
English: You're gonna prove me not guilty today, right?

Japanese: フッフッフッ‥‥ たのみますよ、弁護士センセイ。
English: Hahaha... If you please, Mister Lawyer.

Japanese: アンタの友人のイノチも かかってるんですからねえ‥‥
English: Remember, it's not just me. Your precious friend's life is riding on today's verdict too.

Japanese: くそっ‥‥！
English: Grr...!

Japanese: いい？　王都楼のコト‥‥ かならず有罪にしてやってよね！
English: Now listen up! You'd better get Engarde a guilty sentence, OK!?

Japanese: あんなヒドいヤツを無罪にしたら ‥‥あたし、ユルさないから！
English: If you get that creepy slimebag a not guilty, I'll never forgive you, ever!

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃん‥‥）
なるほどくん。‥‥なるほどくん！
English: Phoenix... Phoenix!

Japanese: あ‥‥千尋さん！
English: M-Mia!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんは‥‥！ 今、どうしているんですか！
English: Maya! How's Maya!?

Japanese: ‥‥わからないわ。
English: ...I don't know.

Japanese: わからない‥‥？
English: You don't know...?

Japanese: きのうから、私のことを 呼び出してくれないの。
English: She hasn't tried to channel me since yesterday.

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さん。 ぼくは、どうすれば‥‥
English: Mia... What... What am I supposed to do...?

Japanese: 前にも言ったはずよ。
English: Well, like I said.

Japanese: ピンチのとき、弁護士ならば ふてぶてしく笑いなさい、って。
English: For a lawyer, the worst of times are when you have to force your biggest smiles.

Japanese: で、でも‥‥！
English: B-But!

Japanese: まだ、あきらめてはダメ。 希望はあるわ。
English: You can't give up! There's still some hope left.

Japanese: ウソです！ そんなの、どこにも‥‥
English: Stop it, please! There's nothing left... Not here, not anywhere...

Japanese: （クソッ！ また、王都楼のヤツ‥‥）
English: (Argh! It's that accursed Engarde again...)

Japanese: うるさいッ！ ‥‥電話なんか、かけてくるな！
English: WILL YOU LEAVE ME ALONE!? Look, don't call me anymore! I mean it!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ヒドいッス。
English: ......You're really mean, pal.

Japanese: うわわ。イトノコさん！ すすす、すみません！
English: Aaah! Gumshoe! I'm really, really sorry!

Japanese: 今、どこにいるんですか？
English: Where are you?

Japanese: ‥‥警官隊にまぜてもらったッス！ 虎狼死家を追っているッス！
English: They let me join the investigation team and we're chasing after De Killer, pal!

Japanese: ‥‥！ 何か、手がかりは‥‥！
English: Then, you have some sort of lead...!?

Japanese: ‥‥すまねッス。 今のところ‥‥ゼロ、ッス。
English: Sorry, but right now we've got zero leads on the guy...

Japanese: だけど‥‥まだまだ、 あきらめちゃダメッス！
English: But we're not going to give up!

Japanese: イトノコさん‥‥
English: Gumshoe...

Japanese: 裁判が終わるまで‥‥ 判決が下されるまでに、
English: Until the trial is over... until the verdict is handed down,

Japanese: ワレワレはなんとしても、 虎狼死家を見つけだすッス！
English: we're going to do everything we can and find De Killer!

Japanese: 真宵さんを救出してしまえば、 王都楼を有罪にできるッス！
English: If we can get Maya out, then you can get Engarde the guilty verdict he deserves, pal!

Japanese: （た、たしかにそうだけど‥‥）
English: (That's true, I could do that if they found Maya first...)

Japanese: いッスか！　だからアンタは、 できるだけ時間をかせぐッス！
English: You got that!? So you have to do whatever you can to make the trial last longer!

Japanese: 時間を‥‥？
English: I have to make the trial last longer?

Japanese: 御剣検事に全力で立ち向かえば、 きっと審理は長びくッス！
English: If you go at Mr. Edgeworth with everything you've got, then you two can draw it out!
(Oh, now I get it...)

Japanese: アンタなら、やれるッス！ ‥‥アンタと、御剣検事なら！
English: I believe in you, pal! You and Mr. Edgeworth can do it!

Japanese: だから！
English: So!

Japanese: ワレワレを信じて ‥‥がんばるッス！
English: Believe in us! We're going to give it all we've got, just like you!

Japanese: わかりました！ ありがとう‥‥イトノコさん！
English: Got it! Thanks Gumshoe!
...Hey, Phoenix.

Japanese: わかったでしょう？ なるほどくん。
English: You understand now, don't you?

Japanese: あなたには、仲間がいるわ。
English: You have something money will never be able to buy. Friendship.

Japanese: おカネでは買うことのできない ‥‥最強の武器よ。
English: It's the strongest weapon in the world and you have it in abundance.

Japanese: ‥‥はい！
English: ...Yeah!

Japanese: （どうやら、作戦は決まった‥‥）
English: (Looks like we're coming to the end...)

Japanese: （裁判を長びかせるんだ！ ‥‥イトノコさんを信じて！）
English: (I have to make the trial last as long as I can! Gumshoe will come through, I know it!)

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第３法廷
English: March 23, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 3

Japanese: これより、王都楼　真悟の 法廷を開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Matt Engarde.

Japanese: 弁護側、準備完了しています。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 検察側‥‥もとより。
English: The prosecution has been ready for a while, Your Honor.

Japanese: きのうの審理は、大きなナゾを 残したまま、終わりました。
English: Now, as I recall, we concluded yesterday's session with a big mystery on our hands.

Japanese: それは‥‥この事件における、 華宮　霧緒の役割です。
English: The mystery being what exactly was Ms. Adrian Andrews' role in this murder?

Japanese: はたして彼女は、犯行に 関係しているのか‥‥？
English: That is to say, is she really connected to the crime itself?

Japanese: 御剣検事。本日の方針を 聞かせていただきましょうか。
English: Mr. Edgeworth, if you would please inform the court of today's proceedings.

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒‥‥
English: Adrian Andrews.

Japanese: 彼女は、殺人現場に手をくわえ、 王都楼に不利な証拠を作った。
English: She forged evidence that threw suspicion onto Mr. Engarde,

Japanese: そして、トノサマンの着ぐるみを 使って、現場から逃走した。
English: and then proceeded to escape the crime scene by wearing a Nickel Samurai costume.

Japanese: その点で、彼女は 罪を逃れることはできない。
English: The guilt of these actions are those from which she cannot escape.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ではやはり、 彼女は有罪、ということに‥‥？
English: Hmm, then you're saying that she is guilty after all...?

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！
English: I'm not finished, Your Honor.

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒は断じて、 殺人には無関係なのだ。
English: Ms. Andrews has nothing to do with committing the actual murder.

Japanese: このカードを ごらんいただきたい。
English: I would like to direct the court's attention to this card.

Japanese: なんですか‥‥？ サザエ‥‥に見えますが。
English: What is that...? It looks like a shell...

Japanese: これは‥‥、ある殺し屋の 名刺のようなものだ。
English: This is the calling card of a certain assassin.

Japanese: こ‥‥コロシヤ、ですって？
English: A-Assassin, you say!?

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオを殺害したのは プロの殺し屋だった！
English: Yes, Juan Corrida was killed by a professional assassin!

Japanese: そして、その依頼人こそが 王都楼　真悟だったのだ！
English: And the person who hired the assassin, his client so to speak, is Matt Engarde!

Japanese: な、なんとも オドロクべき事態ですな‥‥
English: Wh-What a surprising turn of events!

Japanese: ‥‥いつものことだ、裁判長。
English: I would think it's become commonplace by now, Your Honor.

Japanese: （今回にかぎって、 ぼくは知っている）
English: (I know what's going on this time...)

Japanese: （御剣が言ってることは、 すべて正しい‥‥）
English: (So I know that everything Edgeworth has said is true...)

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、 持ちこたえるしかないわ。
English: But we still have to hold out as long as we can.

Japanese: 真宵が救出されるまで、ね。
English: At least, until Maya's safe and sound.

Japanese: どうなっちゃうんでしょう。 今日の法廷‥‥。
English: I wonder how the trial will turn out today...?

Japanese: それでは、 最初の証人を呼んでください。
English: Now then, please call your first witness, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: 被告人の先輩にあたる、 荷星氏を入廷させていただこう。
English: The prosecution calls the defendant's "mentor", Mr. Will Powers to the stand.

Japanese: では、証人‥‥名前と職業を。
English: Now then, witness. Your name and occupation, please.

Japanese: は。きょ、恐縮です。
English: O-OK.

Japanese: ボクはその‥‥ に、荷星　三郎と申しまして。
English: I'm... Uh... Will Powers.

Japanese: ケチなアクション俳優、 やってます。ハイ。
English: I'm a poor, underpaid action star...

Japanese: 被告との関係は？
English: And what is your relation to the defendant?

Japanese: は。その‥‥ケチな先輩、 といったところです。ハイ。
English: Well, that's... I guess I'm sort of a lousy mentor to him in a way. Yeah.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。あまり ヒクツにならないように。
English: Um, Mr. Powers. Please... You don't need to put yourself down so much.

Japanese: は。は‥‥きょ、恐縮です。 なにぶんその。ケチな男なもので。
English: Oh, uh, sorry. Well, but I'm just kind of a nothing sort of guy.

Japanese: 事件当夜。証人は、 被告人の控え室をたずねた。
English: On the night of the murder, you visited the defendant's room.

Japanese: ‥‥まちがいないか？
English: Is this correct?

Japanese: は、ハイ。
English: Y-Yes.

Japanese: ‥‥！ （し、知らなかったぞ‥‥）
English: (I... I didn't know that...)

Japanese: い、イヤ、でも。
English: Um, but you know...

Japanese: ケッキョク、オートロくんには 会えなかったんですよ‥‥
English: I didn't actually get to see Matt when I went...

Japanese: 聞かれたことだけ答えればよい。
English: All you need to do is answer what you're asked.

Japanese: では、そのときのことを 証言していただこう。
English: Now then, I would like you to please testify about when you went to Mr. Engarde's room.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥きょ、恐縮です‥‥。
English: O-OK... Sure...

Japanese: ～控え室をたずねたこと～
English: -- Visit to Matt's Room --

Japanese: 授賞式が終わってから、１人で 控え室へ行ったんです。
English: After the award ceremony, I went by myself to Matt's room.

Japanese: 控え室の前に、オートロくんが。 トノサマンの衣装を着てましたね。
English: Matt was standing there in front of his room, still in his Nickel Samurai costume.

Japanese: だれかと話していたんです。 最初は‥‥ボーイかと思いました。
English: He was talking with someone. At first, I thought it was the bellboy.

Japanese: しばらく２人を見ていましたケド、 あきらめて帰りました。
English: I watched the two of them for a while, but then I gave up and went back.

Japanese: ボクにも、連れがあったので‥‥ 待たせるワケにもいかなくて。
English: I had guests with me that night, and I couldn't make them wait for me.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥特に、あやしい ところはないと思いますが‥‥。
English: Hmm... Nothing sounds out of place in Mr. Powers' testimony.

Japanese: （たしかに。ボーイと話していた から、どうだと言うんだ‥‥？）
English: (And talking with the bellboy is no big deal...)

Japanese: ‥‥そう。それが、ただのボーイ だったのならば、だが。
English: If one assumes that the person Mr. Engarde was speaking with was an ordinary bellboy...

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Wh-What are you implying?

Japanese: さあ、弁護人クン。 ‥‥尋問をおねがいしよう。
English: Well, Mr. Wright. Let's have your cross-examination, shall we?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥ ヤッカイなことになりそうね。
English: Looks like we're in another sticky situation.
Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥ワナよ。 わかりきってるでしょう？
English: A trap. Can't you smell it, Phoenix?

Japanese: でも‥‥情報を得るためには、 飛びこんでいくしかないわね。
English: But for us to find out more, we're just going to have to charge in head first, right?

Japanese: ～控え室をたずねたこと～
English: -- Visit to Matt's Room --

Japanese: 授賞式が終わってから、１人で 控え室へ行ったんです。
English: After the award ceremony, I went by myself to Matt's room.

Japanese: 控え室の前に、オートロくんが。 トノサマンの衣装を着てましたね。
English: Matt was standing there in front of his room, still in his Nickel Samurai costume.

Japanese: しばらく２人を見ていましたケド、 あきらめて帰りました。
English: I watched the two of them for a while, but then I gave up and went back.

Japanese: ボクにも、連れがあったので‥‥ 待たせるワケにもいかなくて。
English: I had guests with me that night, and I couldn't make them wait for me.

Japanese: あなたは‥‥ おぼえているの？
English: Do you remember this incident?

Japanese: あの晩、荷星さんが 席をはずしたこと。
English: Did Mr. Powers leave his seat that night?

Japanese: それが‥‥ ハッキリしないんですよ。
English: I don't remember that happening at all.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん、おおさわぎ してましたから‥‥
English: Maya was making such a racket in her hyper state...

Japanese: それに気をとられていて。
English: I ended up focusing on her.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。
English: ...I see.

Japanese: いずれにせよ‥‥そんなに 長い時間ではなかったわけね。
English: In any case, from his story, he probably wasn't gone for very long.

Japanese: だれかと話していたんです。 最初は‥‥ボーイかと思いました。
English: He was talking with someone. At first, I thought it was the bellboy.

Japanese: オートロくん、ボーイさんに チップをあげていたんですよ。
English: Matt gave the bellboy a tip.

Japanese: 被告の控え室‥‥ 何か用があったんですか？
English: The defendant's room? Why did you go there?

Japanese: それはホラ。センパイとして お祝いのコトバを言おうと‥‥
English: Well, I'm his mentor, like a big brother sort of, and I wanted to say congrats...

Japanese: ど、どうしましたか？ ダマりこんで。
English: Wh-What's wrong? Why did you stop?

Japanese: な、な、な、成歩堂さん！
English: M-M-Mr. Wright!

Japanese: な、な、な、なんですか。
English: Wh-Wh-What is it?

Japanese: ボクを‥‥ ボクをハメるつもりですね！
English: You... You're going to try to trick me into a corner, aren't you?

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ボ‥‥ボク、たしかに ケチなアクション俳優ですけど‥‥
English: I... I know I'm just a poor, underpaid action star, but...

Japanese: でもボクは‥‥ボクは ハンニンなんかじゃない！
English: But... I... I'm not the killer!

Japanese: い、いやいや。ダレも そんなこと言ってませんよ。
English: Um, no one said you were, Mr. Powers.

Japanese: いーや、ダマされません！
English: No, please! Don't trick me!

Japanese: 成歩堂さん、いっつもそうやって、 ヒトをいつのまにかハンニンに‥‥
English: Every time you do your lawyer thing, the witness suddenly turns into the bad guy...

Japanese: （“いっつも”‥‥）
English: ("Every time"...?)

Japanese: ‥‥証人。
English: ...Witness.

Japanese: 弁護人には、あとで私から よく言って聞かせておく。
English: I will personally talk to the defense at a later time.

Japanese: だから今は、おとなしく 証言をつづけていただこう。
English: So for now, please kindly cooperate and continue with your testimony.

Japanese: きょ‥‥恐縮です。
English: S-Sorry...

Japanese: 証人は、被告人の控え室へ 向かった。‥‥そして？
English: So, you went to the defendant's room. And then?

Japanese: （なんだ？　ケッキョク、 ぼくがワルモノなのか‥‥？）
English: (Hey, wait a minute! When and how did I suddenly turn into the bad guy here??)

Japanese: それは、たしかに 王都楼　真悟だったのですか？
English: Are you sure that was Matt Engarde?

Japanese: ええ。まちがいありませんよ。 お面はつけてませんでしたから。
English: Yeah, I'm sure. He wasn't wearing the Nickel Samurai mask then.

Japanese: （それなら‥‥ 見まちがえるワケ、ないか）
English: (If that's the case, then he really can't be mistaken...)

Japanese: ‥‥それで？
English: And?

Japanese: 被告人は、自分の控え室の 前で、何を‥‥？
English: What was the defendant doing, standing in front of his own room?
"At first"? What do you mean by that?

Japanese: ええ。ボーイっぽい服装をして、 お盆にジュースをのせてました。
English: Well, he was in a bellboy-ish uniform and he had a bottle of juice on a tray.

Japanese: ‥‥フツーじゃないですか。
English: ...Sounds like an ordinary bellboy to me.

Japanese: ま。そうですけどね。
English: Um, yeah, but...

Japanese: でも、アレはただのボーイじゃない ‥‥そう思ったんですよ。
English: I didn't think he was a normal bellboy.

Japanese: どうしてですか？
English: And why was that?

Japanese: えーと‥‥どうして でしたっけねえ、成歩堂さん？
English: Um... Why did I think that, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: し、知りませんよ！
English: H-How am I supposed to know!?

Japanese: 恐縮ですが‥‥今はちょっと、 思い出せません。
English: Sorry, but I can't remember right now. ...Sorry.

Japanese: （しかたないな。 もう少し、待ってみるか‥‥）
English: (I guess I'm going to have to wait patiently on this one...)

Japanese: ボーイさんと被告人を 見ていたんですね？
English: You saw the two of them, the bellboy and the defendant, together, correct?

Japanese: ええ。ヒトコト、 お祝いを言いたかったので。
English: Yeah. The bellboy just wanted to say congrats.

Japanese: ２人を見ていて、何か 気づいたコト、ありましたか？
English: Now, while you were watching the two of them, did you notice anything strange?

Japanese: そうですねえ。‥‥なんか、 ミョーな感じがしたんですよ。
English: Um... You know, I did feel something weird.

Japanese: オートロくんが‥‥ボーイさんに チップをあげたから、かなあ。
English: I think it was because Matt... Well, he gave the bellboy a tip.

Japanese: チップ‥‥？ （フツーだと思うけどな）
English: A tip? (But that's a perfectly normal thing to do...)

Japanese: それで、どれぐらい その２人を見ていたのですかな？
English: So, how long did you watch the two of them?

Japanese: いやあ。１、２分ぐらいだと 思いますよ。
English: Ah, not more than a minute or two, I think.

Japanese: “連れ”というのは‥‥
English: So who are these "guests" you're talking about?

Japanese: モチロン、成歩堂さんたちです。 真宵さんと、春美さん。
English: You guys, of course. You and Maya and little Pearl.

Japanese: 招待したボクが消えちゃ イケナイと思ったので‥‥、
English: I thought it would be really rude since I invited you guys, if I disappeared on you...

Japanese: すぐ、席にもどったんです。
English: So I went back to my seat pretty soon after seeing Matt in the hallway.

Japanese: （ここは‥‥ゆさぶっても しょうがない、か）
English: (This is like squeezing water from a stone... It's probably pointless to press further...)
"At first"? What do you mean by that?

Japanese: アレはただのボーイじゃない ‥‥そう思ったんですよ。
English: I didn't think he was a normal bellboy.

Japanese: どうしてですか？
English: And why was that?

Japanese: えーと‥‥どうして でしたっけねえ、成歩堂さん？
English: Um... Why did I think that, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: し、知りませんよ！ （‥‥と、ちょっと待てよ‥‥）
English: H-How am I supposed to know!? (...Hey, wait a second...)

Japanese: そういえば荷星さん、さっき こんなことを言いましたね。
English: Actually, Mr. Powers, only a few minutes ago, you stated:

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、ミョーな感じが したんですよ。
English: Um... You know, I did feel something weird.

Japanese: オートロくんが‥‥ボーイさんに チップをあげたから、かなあ。
English: I think it was because Matt... Well, he gave the bellboy a tip.

Japanese: もしかしたら‥‥チップのことで “おかしい”と感じたのでは‥‥？
English: Could it be that you felt something "strange" about the tip-giving incident itself...?

Japanese: あ！　そうです！　それ！ ‥‥よくわかりましたねえ。
English: AH! Yeah! That's it! You really know your job!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥御剣検事。
English: Hmm... Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだろうか。
English: Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: そのボーイさん‥‥やはり、 タダモノでは‥‥？
English: This bellboy... He wasn't an ordinary one, was he...?

Japanese: それは、証人のコトバが 教えてくれるだろう。
English: Perhaps we should let the witness tell us.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: では、証人。 ‥‥証言を修正してください。
English: Mr. Powers. Please amend your testimony.

Japanese: はあ。ボーイさんの くだりですね？
English: You mean about the bellboy, right?

Japanese: “ボーイにチップをあげた” ‥‥どこがオカシイんですか？
English: So he gave the bellboy a tip. What's so strange about that?

Japanese: いやあ。オートロくん、ボクと ちがってケチじゃないですけど。
English: Ah, well, you see, Matt's not a poor penny-pincher like me.

Japanese: さすがに、アレはどうかと 思って、ビックリしましたね。
English: I was trying to figure out how much it was because the tip really shocked me.
"How much it was"...?

Japanese: それに、もっと おどろいたコトがあったんですよ！
English: But that's when something even more surprising happened!

Japanese: ボーイさん、もらったチップを ポケットにしまったんです。
English: The bellboy was putting the tip he got in his pocket.

Japanese: そのとき、初めて彼のカオが 見えたんですけど‥‥
English: And that's when I got my first good look at the guy's face...

Japanese: それにもビックリしましたね！
English: I was really shocked!

Japanese: ううん‥‥よくわかりませんね。
English: Hmm... I'm afraid I don't follow at all.

Japanese: （どうやら荷星さんは、 ２回ビックリしたらしい‥‥）
English: (It sounds like Mr. Powers was surprised twice by this event.)

Japanese: （どっちのビックリについて 聞いてみようか‥‥？）
English: (I wonder which of his shocking moments I should ask about...?)

Japanese: ボーイさんのカオが どうかしたんですか？
English: What was so shocking about the bellboy's face, Mr. Powers?

Japanese: そもそも“ボーイ”というより “じいや”って感じでしたけど‥‥
English: Well, he wasn't exactly a "boy"... more like an old "gramps"...

Japanese: あ。コラ！　おジイさんを サベツするつもりなら‥‥
English: Ahem! I hope you know that discrimination towards old men is a no-no in my court!

Japanese: い、いえいえ。そうじゃ ありません。
English: No, no, that's not what I meant at all!

Japanese: カオのド真ん中に‥‥ ぬい目があったんですよ！
English: In the smack middle of the guy's face, there was a line of stitches!
A line of stitches...?

Japanese: それも、ヒタイの上から アゴの下までですよ！
English: Yeah! And it went straight from the tippy top of his head to the bottom of his chin!

Japanese: もし糸が切れたら、中の ツメモノが流れだしてきそうな‥‥
English: Almost like if that thread snapped, all the stuff in his head would come spilling out.

Japanese: あッ！
English: Ah!

Japanese: （王都楼の家にいた‥‥ あの執事！）
English: (He was there... At Engarde's house... He was that BUTLER!)

Japanese: なんですか？
English: What is it, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: い、いえッ！
English: A-Ah, nothing, Your Honor!

Japanese: （‥‥王都楼が虎狼死家と 話していた‥‥）
English: (So that means Engarde was talking with De Killer then.)

Japanese: （そんなことがバレたら‥‥ アッという間に有罪判決だ！）
English: (If that fact were to be exposed, Engarde would be declared guilty in a blink!)

Japanese: ここはトボけておきなさい！ なるほどくん！
English: Phoenix, you have to play dumb here! Pretend you don't know anything!

Japanese: はいッ！
English: Yes, Chief!

Japanese: どうかしましたか？　弁護人。
English: You sure you don't have something you would like to say, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥はい？　なんのことでしょう？
English: Huh? Umm... What did you just say, Your Honor?

Japanese: なんでもありません。
English: Nothing, Mr. Wright. Nothing.

Japanese: どうやら、ずいぶん回り道を してしまったようですな。
English: We're just going around and around in circles.

Japanese: ‥‥では、証人。 証言をつづけなさい。
English: Now then, Mr. Powers. Please continue with your testimony.

Japanese: 被告人は大スターです。 チップぐらいバラまくでしょう！
English: The defendant is a huge star. He can afford to give generous tips, wouldn't you agree?

Japanese: まあ、そうですケド。
English: Um, sure.

Japanese: さすがにアレはちょっと、 バラまきすぎなのでは‥‥
English: But giving him that much was maybe a little too much, I think...

Japanese: （“バラまきすぎ”‥‥？）
English: ("A little too much"...?)

Japanese: ‥‥ハッキリさせよう。
English: Would you please clarify for the court,

Japanese: 被告人は、ボーイに いくらわたしたのか？
English: about how much would you say the defendant gave to the bellboy?

Japanese: ちゃんとした 金額はわかりません。
English: Honestly, I don't know. I can't even begin to guess.

Japanese: どうしてですか？
English: And why is that?

Japanese: だって‥‥ ぶあつい札束だったんですから。
English: Because he gave the bellboy a really, really fat roll of cash.

Japanese: さささ、サツタバあ！
English: A ROLL OF CASH!?

Japanese: あー‥‥たしかにその。
English: Ah, well... How interesting...

Japanese: ケタはずれですねえ。 ‥‥チップとしては。
English: That certainly was a very generous tip, wasn't it?

Japanese: ぶあつい札束‥‥それは、 チップとは考えられない！
English: A very fat roll of cash... That can hardly be called a "tip", Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: （裁判長、ウタガイのマナコで こっちを見てるぞ‥‥）
English: (The judge is beginning to look awfully suspicious of us...)

Japanese: 被告はスーパースターだった！
English: The defendant is a superstar!

Japanese: それぐらいのチップは その。トーゼンです！
English: That kind of tip is typical fare for people like him!

Japanese: スーパースターは、アタマの中も スーパーだと言うのか！
English: Are you saying that all superstars are super- spenders!?

Japanese: お盆を運んでサツタバが もらえるのならば‥‥！
English: If I could receive large rolls of cash by simply bringing people things on trays...

Japanese: ダレが検事などやるものかッ！
English: Then why on Earth would I stand around here prosecuting!?

Japanese: （‥‥スゴいこと言ってるな‥‥）
English: (He's got a point... I don't even get paid, let alone rolls of cash for all my hard work.)

Japanese: （ここで“異議”はないだろう）
English: (There's nothing I can really object to here.)

Japanese: （たしかに、チップに札束は 異常だからな‥‥）
English: (I mean, who can argue that a fat roll of money isn't really odd...?)

Japanese: しかし、その札束が チップでなかったとしたら‥‥
English: Hmm, so supposing that roll of cash was not a tip...

Japanese: いったい、なんだったのですか？
English: Then what was it?

Japanese: 依頼料だったのだ。
English: Payment, Your Honor.

Japanese: いらい‥‥？
English: Payment...?

Japanese: “藤見野　イサオの殺害”に 決まっているではないか。
English: Isn't it obvious? For the murder of Mr. Juan Corrida.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、じゃあ‥‥ 証人が見た、そのボーイは‥‥
English: Then... Then the bellboy the witness saw...

Japanese: そう。殺し屋だったのだ。
English: Yes, he was the assassin.

Japanese: ま、待ってください！ そんな‥‥証拠もなく‥‥！
English: H-Hold your horses now! Mr. Edgeworth, you don't have any proof of this... do you?

Japanese: ‥‥私に、証拠なき発言は あり得ない。
English: Have I ever been unprepared to support my claims, Your Honor?

Japanese: 虎狼死家　左々右エ門が 今回の現場に残したカードがある。
English: I have here, the card Shelly de Killer left at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: ころしや‥‥さざえもん‥‥
English: Shelly... de Killer...

Japanese: 警視庁の特別捜査課が、 ずっと追っている人物だ。
English: He is the person the police's special investigations team has been chasing for ages.

Japanese: 証人が見たボーイは、 殺し屋の虎狼死家だったのだ！
English: I am certain that the person the witness saw was this very assassin, Shelly de Killer!

Japanese: そ‥‥そうだったんですかッ！
English: R-Really!?

Japanese: ‥‥？ 証人。どうしましたか？
English: What's wrong, Mr. Powers?

Japanese: いや、それならナットクできる コトがあるんですよ！
English: No, nothing. Something just clicked in my head and I think I just figured something out!

Japanese: ほ、ほお‥‥
English: Oh?

Japanese: じつはボク、そのボーイさんを 見てるんです！　もう一度。
English: Actually, I saw that bellboy again later on that night!

Japanese: なんですってェェ！
English: WHAAAT!?

Japanese: それを証言してください！ ‥‥あなたの見たことを！
English: Mr. Powers! Please testify! Tell us what you saw!

Japanese: はいッ！　恐縮ですッ！
English: Yes, sir! Right away!

Japanese: ～二度目の目撃～
English: -- The Second Time --

Japanese: 今度はトイレに行きたくなって、 あのろうかへ行ったんですよ！
English: This time, I was in that hallway because I had to go to the bathroom!

Japanese: そうしたら‥‥さっきのボーイが 控え室から出てきたんですよ！
English: And that's when that bellboy I saw earlier came out of the room!

Japanese: もちろん、藤見野　イサオくんの 控え室からですよ！
English: Of course, when I say "room", I mean Juan Corrida's room!

Japanese: 今、思えばあのボーイさん、 やっぱり不自然でしたよ！
English: Now that I think about it, that bellboy did seem kinda out of place!

Japanese: そうですよ！　アイツ、 殺し屋だったんです、きっと！
English: Yeah! So he had to be the assassin! I'm sure of it!

Japanese: だって‥‥
English: I mean...

Japanese: とりあえず、そこまでで よろしい。
English: Thank you very much. That is all we need for now.
Huh?

Japanese: でも、まだ話は 終わってませんケド‥‥。
English: But I'm not done. There's still more...

Japanese: まずは、そのボーイが虎狼死家 だったことを立証しておきたい。
English: Let us first establish that the bellboy was truly Mr. de Killer. Then we shall see.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥被害者の控え室から ボーイさんが‥‥
English: Hmm... So the bellboy came out of the victim's room...

Japanese: もし、そのボーイが 殺し屋だったのなら‥‥
English: And if that bellboy really was the assassin...

Japanese: ‥‥もう、決定的ですね。
English: Then, I think the answer is fairly obvious.

Japanese: そういうこと、だ。
English: That would be correct, Your Honor.

Japanese: では弁護人、出番だ。 ‥‥せいぜい笑わせてくれたまえ。
English: Well, Mr. Wright. I believe it's your turn... to entertain and make us laugh.

Japanese: ‥‥はははは。
English: Ha. Ha. Ha. Ha.

Japanese: あなたが笑ってどうするのっ！
English: This is no laughing matter!

Japanese: ～二度目の目撃～
English: -- The Second Time --

Japanese: 今度はトイレに行きたくなって、 あのろうかへ行ったんですよ！
English: This time, I was in that hallway because I had to go to the bathroom!

Japanese: そうしたら‥‥さっきのボーイが 控え室から出てきたんですよ！
English: And that's when that bellboy I saw earlier came out of the room!

Japanese: もちろん、藤見野　イサオくんの 控え室からですよ！
English: Of course, when I say "room", I mean Juan Corrida's room!

Japanese: そうですよ！　アイツ、 殺し屋だったんです、きっと！
English: Yeah! So he had to be the assassin! I'm sure of it!

Japanese: とりあえず、そのボーイさんが 殺し屋だということは‥‥
English: In any case, if that bellboy was the assassin,

Japanese: 認めちゃマズいわね。
English: it would be very bad for us.

Japanese: でも‥‥ホントは 殺し屋だったんですよね。
English: But... he really is the assassin, you know?

Japanese: そうよ。‥‥でも。
English: Yes... But.

Japanese: カンタンに認めちゃダメ。 ‥‥時間をかせぐのなら‥‥
English: You can't give in yet. If you want to prolong this trial for as long as possible,

Japanese: なんとかして、証言にケチを つけるしかないわね。
English: you're going to have to pull some cheap tricks on this one.

Japanese: 今、思えばあのボーイさん、 やっぱり不自然でしたよ！
English: Now that I think about it, that bellboy did seem kinda out of place!

Japanese: お客の部屋から手ぶらで出てくる なんて、おかしいですよね！
English: I thought it was kinda strange for a bellboy to come out of a guest's room empty-handed!

Japanese: 時間はわかりますか？
English: And what time was it?

Japanese: いやー、 ハッキリしないですねえ。
English: Ah, well, I don't remember.

Japanese: 授賞式が終わったのが ８時ごろでしたよね。
English: The award ceremony ended around 8 PM, right?

Japanese: ‥‥すぐに控え室に行って、 帰ってきて‥‥
English: ...And I went to Matt's room pretty soon after that... And then I came back...

Japanese: またトイレに、と。
English: And then I went to the bathroom...

Japanese: たぶん、８時１０分ぐらい ですかねえ。
English: So I guess maybe it was around 8:10 PM by that time?

Japanese: ずいぶん落ちつきのない ヒトですねえ、あなた。
English: You're not one for details, are you Mr. Powers?

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です。
English: S-Sorry...

Japanese: オートロくんに会えるかも しれない、と思ったもので‥‥。
English: I thought I could maybe catch Matt and say my congrats...

Japanese: 本当に、同じボーイさんでしたか？
English: Are you sure it was the same bellboy?

Japanese: ええ。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: どうしてわかるんですか？
English: And how could you tell?

Japanese: ボーイさんなんて、みんな 同じ制服を着ていますよ！
English: All the bellboys wear the same uniform, after all!

Japanese: でも、ホラ。ぬい目が ありましたから。カオに。
English: But, you see... Well, he had those stitches in his face.

Japanese: ぐ。
English: Urk.

Japanese: まちがいなく、オートロくんと 話していたボーイさんです。
English: So I'm sure it's the same guy that was talking with Matt.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: それで？　そのボーイは どの控え室から‥‥？
English: So which room did the bellboy come out of?

Japanese: 被害者の控え室‥‥ですか。
English: The victim's room, huh...?

Japanese: ええ。ゴウカなお花に クマのぬいぐるみ。
English: Yeah. The one with all the really pretty flowers and teddy bears.

Japanese: イサオくんの楽屋に まちがいありません！
English: It was Juan's room alright!

Japanese: （ううう‥‥コトバを挟む 余地がないぞ）
English: (Words cannot describe how screwed I am...)

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: では、証言を つづけていただこうか。
English: Let's continue with the testimony, shall we?

Japanese: ‥‥あの‥‥ 何が不自然だったんですか？
English: Um... so what exactly was so "out of place" about him?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ、その へっぴり腰なタイドは。
English: Wright, Wright, Wright. Why the insipid grin?

Japanese: （だって‥‥何が出てくるか わからないし‥‥）
English: (Maybe because I have no idea what damaging thing he's going to say next...?)

Japanese: いやあ、そのボーイさん ‥‥手ぶらだったんですよ。
English: Um, well, the bellboy was empty-handed.

Japanese: 手ぶら‥‥？
English: Empty-handed?

Japanese: あのボーイさん、ルームサービスの 係のヒトですよね？
English: That bellboy was one of those room-service people, right?

Japanese: それなのに、ワゴンも押さず、 お盆も持っていなかったんですよ。
English: But he wasn't pushing a cart and he wasn't holding a tray either!

Japanese: ね。ね。これってやっぱり、 不自然ですよね？
English: You'd call that "a little strange" too, wouldn't you?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥どうも、 ビミョーなところですなあ‥‥
English: Hmm... I agree that it is a bit strange, Mr. Powers.

Japanese: （ボーイが手ぶらだった‥‥。 そんなに不自然なのかな）
English: (But is it really that unusual for a bellboy to be empty- handed?)

Japanese: （どうする？　荷星さんの証言、 そのまま認めるべきか‥‥？）
English: (What should I do? Should I let Mr. Powers' testimony slide or...?)

Japanese: そうですね。 ‥‥たしかに不自然だと思います。
English: You're right. I think it's pretty unusual too.

Japanese: やっぱり！　成歩堂さんも そう思いますよね！
English: Ah, I thought you might think so!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: フッ。‥‥弁護人がスナオに 認めるなら、言うことはない。
English: Hmph. There's no need to say anything when the defense gives up without a fight.

Japanese: ‥‥先に進めていただこう。
English: Let's move on.

Japanese: とにかく証人は、そのボーイを アヤシイと思ったわけですね？
English: Anyway, Mr. Powers. You thought the bellboy was a little suspicious, correct?

Japanese: 手ぶらのボーイ‥‥ ゼンゼンおかしくありません！
English: There is nothing strange or unusual about an empty- handed bellboy!

Japanese: すごく不自然ですよ！
English: But there really, really is!

Japanese: ごく自然です！
English: There really, really isn't!

Japanese: ボーイは、ルームサービスの 係だったのだ。
English: If you two are done being schoolchildren...

Japanese: 何か理由がないかぎり、 手ぶらというのはオカシイ‥‥。
English: Bellboys are for room service. There is no reason for them to be empty-handed, ever.

Japanese: 裁判長！　今の証人の発言を、 証言として採用していただきたい。
English: Your Honor! I ask that the witness' previous statement be supplanted with this new one.

Japanese: （御剣のヤツ‥‥）
English: (Grr... Edgeworth...)

Japanese: （なんとしても、そのボーイを 疑わしく見せようというワケか）
English: (Are you going to do whatever you can to make the bellboy look suspicious?)

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 検察側の主張を認めましょう。
English: I see. Very well, this court recognizes and grants the prosecution's request.

Japanese: では、証人。 証言を修正してください。
English: Mr. Powers, if you could amend your testimony, please.

Japanese: は。きょ、恐縮です。
English: Y-Yes, sir.

Japanese: そんなカンタンに 決めつけないでください！
English: Please don't be so quick to judge!

Japanese: あ。でも、荷星家の 家訓なんですよ。
English: Ah, but it's kind of a Powers family thing.

Japanese: “ヒトを見たら ドロボーと思え”って。
English: "Think of every person as a thief".

Japanese: （“ころしや”と“どろぼー”‥‥ “ろ”しか合ってないぞ）
English: (Well, I guess a thief and an assassin are both sneaky and silent...)

Japanese: そういう問題じゃないわ、 なるほどくん。
English: That's not the point, Phoenix.

Japanese: 手ぶらがアヤシイんですか？
English: So you're saying that it's suspicious for him to be empty-handed?

Japanese: そりゃ、アヤシイと 思いますよ。
English: Yeah, really suspicious.

Japanese: だって、あのボーイを 最初に見たときだって‥‥
English: I mean, when I first saw that bellboy,

Japanese: 彼、ちゃんと お盆をもってましたからね！
English: he was holding a tray in his hand!

Japanese: お盆には、ジュースのボトルと グラスが乗っていましたよ。
English: And there was a bottle of juice and a wine glass on it!

Japanese: ジュース‥‥。 なんのジュースでしたか？
English: Juice...? What kind of juice was it?

Japanese: たしか‥‥トマトジュース だったと思いますけど。
English: Um, I'm pretty sure it was tomato juice...

Japanese: “ボーイさんが手ぶらだった理由” ‥‥それを示す証拠があれば、
English: If we could come up with some sort of reason as to why he would come out empty-handed,

Japanese: ‥‥とりあえず、 急場はしのげるみたいね。
English: some sort of proof, then I think we can dodge the bullet on this one for now.

Japanese: 手ぶらだった理由、ですか。 （見てみるか‥‥法廷記録）
English: Proof, huh? (Sounds like another job for the Court Record!)

Japanese: ‥‥荷星さん。
English: Mr. Powers.

Japanese: なな、なんですか。
English: Y-Y-Yes?

Japanese: あなたは、すぐに ヒトのコトバに影響されますね。
English: You're easily influenced by other people's words, aren't you?

Japanese: “ボーイが殺人者かもしれない” と聞いただけで、
English: As soon as you heard that the bellboy might have been the killer,

Japanese: すっかりそれを 信じこんでしまう‥‥。
English: you got caught up in believing it must be true.

Japanese: だって‥‥だって、 アヤシイじゃないですか！
English: But... But... Isn't he really suspicious!?

Japanese: ぬい目があったんですよ？ あのボーイには！
English: He's got all those stitches, and... and...

Japanese: ぬい目ぐらい、 野球のボールにだってある！
English: So? A baseball has stitches!

Japanese: 野球のボールが アヤシイと言うつもりですかッ！
English: Are you saying all baseballs are suspicious because they have stitches!?

Japanese: わぐ！
English: *gulp!*

Japanese: それに‥‥そう！ 手ぶらですよ！　ボーイなのに。
English: Well, there's also... I mean, what about him being empty-handed!?

Japanese: ‥‥この写真を見てください。
English: I would like to ask the court to please take a look here.

Japanese: これは‥‥殺人現場の‥‥
English: This is... the crime scene...

Japanese: イサオさんの死体のそばには グラスが置かれていました。
English: There is a wine glass sitting next to Mr. Corrida's body.

Japanese: ‥‥中身は、トマトジュースです。
English: The liquid inside this glass is tomato juice.

Japanese: そして、右下に写っている テーブルの上を見てください。
English: And now, if you would look at what is on top of the table in the lower right corner here...

Japanese: これはどう見ても、お盆と トマトジュースのボトルです！
English: Anyone can clearly see that it is a tray with a bottle of tomato juice on it!

Japanese: ボーイさんは、これを イサオさんに届けたばかりだった。
English: The bellboy had just brought this to Mr. Corrida's room.

Japanese: お盆は、部屋に置いてきた。 ‥‥だから、手ぶらだったんです。
English: He left the tray in the room, which is why he was empty- handed when he left!

Japanese: わぐ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: し、しかし！
English: B-But!

Japanese: そのボーイさんは、死体があった 控え室から出てきたのですぞ！
English: That would mean that the bellboy had seen and left a dead body in the room!

Japanese: そのとき、イサオさんが死んで いたかどうか、立証できますか？
English: Ah, but can you prove that Mr. Corrida was already dead at that time?

Japanese: うぐ。‥‥み、御剣検事ッ！
English: Uh... M-Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: ‥‥うム。
English: ...Yes?

Japanese: あなたのせいで、この私が やりこめられたではないですかッ！
English: I-I blame you for leading me down this route!

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ。 もうしわけない‥‥。
English: Heh heh heh. I'm terribly sorry...

Japanese: （なんだ？　笑ってるぞ‥‥）
English: (What is with him? Why is he laughing??)

Japanese: ‥‥証人。 １つ、うかがいたいことがある。
English: Witness. Isn't there one more thing you would like to share with us?

Japanese: は、はい。なんでしょう‥‥？
English: I-Is there?

Japanese: “ボーイは手ぶらだった” ‥‥その“手”についてだが。
English: The bellboy was empty- handed... Or should I say empty-"hand"ed?

Japanese: 私が話を聞いたときは、 たしか、その“手”には‥‥
English: I recall you had something interesting to say about his hands...

Japanese: ああッ！ そうだ、言い忘れてました！
English: Oh yeah! I almost forgot!

Japanese: え。‥‥な、ナニを‥‥？
English: Huh? Wh-What...?

Japanese: そのボーイさん、 手袋をしていたんですよ。
English: That bellboy -- he was wearing gloves!

Japanese: てぶくろ‥‥？
English: Gloves?

Japanese: まっ黒な、革の手袋です。
English: Yeah, pitch black, leather ones.

Japanese: フツー、ボーイさんは そんな手袋、しないですよね？
English: All the other bellboys don't wear gloves like that, right?

Japanese: ‥‥黒い‥‥革の手袋‥‥
English: Black leather gloves...

Japanese: なんでそれを先に 言わなかったのですかッ！
English: Why didn't you mention them earlier!?

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です‥‥
English: S-Sorry... It slipped my mind.

Japanese: （くそッ！　たしかに すごくアヤシイけど‥‥）
English: (Argh! Boy does this make the bellboy look really suspicious...)

Japanese: （ここで引っこむワケには いかない‥‥！）
English: (Alright, got to focus. I can't get lax here!)

Japanese: ‥‥手袋ぐらい、 したっていいでしょう！
English: So what if he had gloves? A lot of bellboys wear gloves!

Japanese: だって、革の手袋ですよ！ ボーイなのに！
English: Come on, Mr. Wright! That bellboy was wearing black leather ones!

Japanese: 革でできてるといえば、 野球のボールだってそうです！
English: So? A football is made of leather!

Japanese: 野球のボールが アヤシイと言うつもりですかッ！
English: Are you saying all footballs are suspicious because they are made of leather!?

Japanese: わぐ！
English: *gulp!*

Japanese: ‥‥その男は、被告人から 札束を受けとりました。
English: But that man... He received a large roll of cash from the defendant.

Japanese: そして、殺害現場から、 黒い革手袋をはめて出てきた。
English: And then he was seen leaving the crime scene wearing black leather gloves.

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、ただのボーイとは 考えにくいですね‥‥
English: I don't think that even some- one like myself can believe he was just another bellboy...

Japanese: ぐぐ‥‥。
English: Urgh...

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら おわかりいただけたようだ‥‥
English: It seems that we have finally come to an understanding...

Japanese: では、証人。 証言のつづきをおねがいしたい。
English: Now then, witness. Please continue with the rest of your testimony.

Japanese: つづき‥‥だと？
English: The rest...?

Japanese: ほお‥‥それはぜひ、 聞かせていただきたいですな。
English: Oh yes, please tell us more.

Japanese: ‥‥恐縮ですッ！
English: OK!

Japanese: ～被告とボーイ、二度目の接触～
English: -- Their Second Meeting --

Japanese: ボーイは、そのままオートロくんの 控え室のドアをノックしたんです。
English: After leaving Juan's room, the bellboy went and knocked on Matt's door, just like that.

Japanese: そして、部屋の中のヒトに、 何やら手わたしたんですよ。
English: He gave something to the person inside the room.

Japanese: おジイさん、そのまま部屋には 入らず、帰っていきました。
English: Then the old guy just left, without even going into the room.

Japanese: それで、ボクもトイレへ 行ってから、席へもどりました。
English: After that, I went to the bathroom and then back to my seat.

Japanese: 殺害現場から出てきたボーイが、 今度は被告人の控え室に‥‥？
English: So the bellboy, after leaving the crime scene, next went to the defendant's room...?

Japanese: そうです。たまたま ‥‥見ちゃったんですよ。
English: Yeah. I kinda saw all that by accident...

Japanese: （それにしても、ちょっと 目撃されすぎだろう！）
English: (Some accident! I'd say you saw too much!)

Japanese: （それも、あやしいトコ ばっかり‥‥）
English: (And all of it was suspicious to high heaven...)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: いよいよ、ただのボーイとは 考えられなくなりましたな。
English: I think it's safe to say that we can no longer consider this bellboy to be "normal".

Japanese: では、弁護人。尋問を。
English: Now then, let's get started, shall we? Mr. Wright, your cross-examination, please.

Japanese: はい‥‥。
English: Yes, Your Honor...

Japanese: ～被告とボーイ、二度目の接触～
English: -- Their Second Meeting --

Japanese: ボーイは、そのままオートロくんの 控え室のドアをノックしたんです。
English: After leaving Juan's room, the bellboy went and knocked on Matt's door, just like that.

Japanese: そして、部屋の中のヒトに、 何やら手わたしたんですよ。
English: He gave something to the person inside the room.

Japanese: おジイさん、そのまま部屋には 入らず、帰っていきました。
English: Then the old guy just left, without even going into the room.

Japanese: それで、ボクもトイレへ 行ってから、席へもどりました。
English: After that, I went to the bathroom and then back to my seat.

Japanese: あいかわらず、ハッキリしませんね ‥‥荷星さんの証言。
English: Well, Mr. Powers' testimony just now was just as vague as his first.

Japanese: ‥‥やっかいね。
English: It's a little troublesome, isn't it?

Japanese: ゆさぶればゆさぶるほど、 キオクがハッキリしてくる。
English: But I'm sure if you press him enough, everything will become clearer.

Japanese: ‥‥しかも、私たちにとって かならず不利な方向に、ね。
English: Although, that just makes it harder on us, doesn't it?

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Ugh... Talk about a lose-lose situation.

Japanese: もう一度見ればハッキリしますけど ‥‥木製の置物みたいでしたね。
English: If I saw it again, I could say for sure, but I think it was some sort of wooden statue.

Japanese: それを、のぞき見していた わけですか？
English: Is that what you saw while you were busy spying?

Japanese: し、シツレイですね！
English: E-Excuse me!?

Japanese: たしかにボクはケチな アクション俳優ですけど、
English: I may be a poor, underpaid action star,

Japanese: ケチなのぞき屋なんかじゃ ありませんよ！
English: but even I wouldn't stoop to spying!

Japanese: ところで、証人は どこからそれを‥‥？
English: Well, I think the point is where did you watch all this from, Mr. Powers?

Japanese: は。トイレの入り口あたりから、 左目だけ、そっとのぞかせて‥‥
English: Oh, um, from the door of the bathroom with my left eye, in a sort of sneaky, spy-like...

Japanese: （リッパなのぞき屋じゃないか！）
English: (I knew he was spying!)

Japanese: ‥‥ケチだろうがリッパだろうが、 そんなことはどうでもよい。
English: Please, does it really matter if he was doing it over or underhandedly?

Japanese: そのボーイは、次に何をしたか？ ‥‥それを証言してほしい。
English: What did the bellboy do next? That's all I care to know.

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: I said, "Hold it!"

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか？
English: Umm... OK.

Japanese: こっちが“なんですか？”ですよ！
English: That's better! *ahem*

Japanese: “中のヒトに何かわたした”‥‥ もっと、ハッキリしてください！
English: What kind of statement is that!? Please elaborate and give us a few more details!

Japanese: そ、そうですねえ‥‥
English: Oh, umm... OK...

Japanese: （荷星さんのアタマは、同時に ２つのコトは処理できない！）
English: (Hmm, I should probably ask him only one question at a time...)

Japanese: （‥‥荷星さんに、これ以上の 情報はキタイできない）
English: (I don't think I can find out much more from Mr. Powers...)

Japanese: （他のことを聞いてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should probably move on to a different topic...)

Japanese: ‥‥その後、ボーイは どうしましたか？
English: So what did the bellboy do after that?

Japanese: ボーイから“何やら受けとった” のは、だれだったんですか？
English: So who took this "something" the bellboy handed off?

Japanese: はあ‥‥それが。 ハッキリしないんですよ。
English: Um, actually, I don't know.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: その。ザンネンながら、 ウデしか見えなかったんです。
English: I'm sorry, but I only saw the person's arm.

Japanese: ウデだけ、ですか‥‥
English: Only an arm...?

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥カオは 見ていないんですね？
English: Then you're saying you didn't see the person's face?

Japanese: ええ。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: まあ‥‥王都楼さんの控え室 だったのですからねえ。
English: Well, it was Mr. Engarde's room, correct?

Japanese: やはり、王都楼さん だったのではないでしょうか。
English: So it could have only been Mr. Engarde himself, I'd say.

Japanese: それで‥‥そのボーイはそれから、 どうしたのか？
English: And then? What did the bellboy do after that?

Japanese: ハイ。部屋の中のヒトに 何やらわたしたあとは‥‥、
English: Oh, so after he gave the person inside the room the thing...

Japanese: “何やら”手わたした‥‥？
English: He gave "something" to this person?

Japanese: ハイ。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: なんだったんですか？
English: And what was this "something"?

Japanese: ハッハッハッ。
English: Hah hah hah.

Japanese: それをおぼえていたら、 “何やら”なんて言いませんよ。
English: If I remembered what it was, I wouldn't be calling it a "something", would I?

Japanese: しかし、それは殺人現場から 持ち出してきたモノと考えられる！
English: But this implies that some- thing was removed from the scene of the crime!

Japanese: ‥‥思い出せませんか？
English: Are you sure you really can't remember, Mr. Powers?

Japanese: うう‥‥何か、小さなモノ だったような‥‥
English: Umm... I think it was something kinda small...

Japanese: ‥‥これは、非常に 重要なポイントと考えられる。
English: This is an incredibly crucial piece of information.

Japanese: ぜひ、思い出して いただきたい。
English: Please, try to remember what it was.

Japanese: うう‥‥がんばってみます。
English: Umm... I'll try...

Japanese: では、他のことを聞こう。 ‥‥そのボーイの行動について。
English: In the meantime, let's talk of another point. Namely, what the bellboy did next.

Japanese: ハイ。部屋の中のヒトに 何やらわたしたあとは‥‥、
English: Oh, so after he gave the person inside the room the thing...

Japanese: どこへ 帰っていったのですか？
English: Where did this bellboy go after he left Mr. Engarde's room?

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。《すみれの間》に 通じるドアを開けて、
English: Hmm... He opened the door to Viola Hall,

Japanese: そこから 行ってしまいましたね。
English: went in there, and who knows after that, right?

Japanese: （ふうん‥‥）
English: (Nngh... I do...)

Japanese: そのとき、何かアヤシイものは 見なかったんですか？
English: Did you see anything strange, suspicious, or just out of the ordinary at that time?

Japanese: いやー、それが見たんですよ。
English: Oh yeah, I saw that one thing!

Japanese: ‥‥え！ （まだ、何か見てるのか‥‥！）
English: What!? (He saw something else!?)

Japanese: それが、光線銃を持った 宇宙人でして‥‥
English: There was this jittery alien with a ray gun...

Japanese: イサオくんの控え室のドアを ちらちら見ていました。
English: It was watching Juan's door like some sort of stalker.

Japanese: ‥‥その宇宙人は ほっておきましょう。
English: Um, I think we can forget about the alien...

Japanese: ‥‥一度、今の証言を 整理させていただこう。
English: I would like to summarize the testimony up to this point, if you don't mind.

Japanese: ボーイは、殺害現場から出てきて、 被告人の控え室へ向かった。
English: When the bellboy left the crime scene, he immediately went to the defendant's room.

Japanese: そこで、部屋の中の人物に 何か小さいモノを手わたした。
English: There, he handed a small item of some sort to the person inside.

Japanese: 受けとった人物については、 ウデしか見えなかった‥‥。
English: As for the person who received the item, all you could see was the person's arm...

Japanese: ええ、ええ！　そんな感じです。
English: Yes, yes! It was just like that!

Japanese: 御剣検事。‥‥それが、 重要なことなのですか？
English: Mr. Edgeworth. Is all this really that important?

Japanese: モチロン‥‥、かなり 重要と考えられる。
English: Of course, Your Honor. I think this is of the utmost importance.

Japanese: なにしろ、殺害現場から持ち出した ものを、依頼主にわたした瞬間だ！
English: This is when whatever was removed from the crime scene was handed over to the client!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 証人。ボーイがわたしたもの ‥‥よく思い出してください。
English: Mr. Powers, please, try to remember what it was the bellboy handed off.

Japanese: そうですねえ‥‥ たしか、あれは‥‥
English: Um... Well, let's see... Hmm... I think it was... No...

Japanese: 思い出したら、証言に つけくわえてください。
English: If you remember, please add it to your testimony.

Japanese: はあ‥‥きょ、恐縮です。
English: Y-Yes, sir.

Japanese: 置物‥‥ですか？
English: A statue?

Japanese: ええ‥‥なんか、 そんなフンイキだったような。
English: Yeah... It kinda looked like one I guess.

Japanese: たぶん、実物を見れば、 思い出せるんですけどねえ。
English: If I saw the actual thing again, I'd probably remember, you know?

Japanese: （審理を先に進めるためには‥‥）
English: (Looks like for this trial to proceed...)

Japanese: （この“置物”の正体を、ハッキリ させる必要があるみたいだ‥‥）
English: (I'm going to have to come up with whatever this "statue" thing is and show it to him.)

Japanese: これは‥‥“賭け”ね。 なるほどくん。
English: You're going to have to trust your instinct on this one and take a chance, Phoenix.

Japanese: ‥‥では、証人。 つづけてください。
English: Well Mr. Powers, let's continue with your testimony.

Japanese: ボーイは、そのあと どうしましたか？
English: What did the bellboy do after that?

Japanese: なんなのだッ！ この気まずい“間”は！
English: What was the point of that pregnant pause!?

Japanese: 今、異議をとなえたヒト！ だれですか？
English: Where did that objection come from!? Well, speak up!

Japanese: はあ。ぼくですけど‥‥。
English: Uh, it was me, Your Honor...

Japanese: どうしたの？　なるほどくん。
English: What is it, Phoenix?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだか、イヤなことが 起こるような気がして‥‥。
English: I have a feeling that something bad is going to happen once I show this...

Japanese: 弁護人！　反証するなら、 さっさとおねがいします！
English: Mr. Wright! If you have something to say, please spit it out!

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 （カクゴを決めるか‥‥）
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor. (OK Phoenix, deep breath...)

Japanese: ‥‥荷星さん。
English: Mr. Powers.

Japanese: あなたが見た“何やら”は‥‥ もしかして、コイツですか？
English: The "something" you saw... Was it this item?

Japanese: あっ！　それだ！　それですよ！
English: Oh, hey! That's it! That's the something!

Japanese: ‥‥いやあ、さすが成歩堂さん。 よくわかりましたねえ。
English: Wow, Mr. Wright. You really figured it out.

Japanese: ‥‥こいつはたしか、 王都楼　真悟の屋敷で見つけた‥‥
English: Hmm, I recall we found this at Matt Engarde's mansion...

Japanese: ひ‥‥被告人の家ですって！
English: At the d-defendant's house!?

Japanese: い、いったいこれは、 どういうことですかッ！
English: What does this mean!?

Japanese: カンタンなことだ‥‥。
English: It's simple, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥虎狼死家は、控え室で 藤見野　イサオを殺害した。
English: Shelly de Killer assassinated Juan Corrida in his room.

Japanese: その際、現場から この木製のクマを盗み出したのだ。
English: And then he stole this wooden bear from the scene of the crime.

Japanese: そのクマが、王都楼氏の屋敷から 発見されたということは‥‥
English: Then, the bear being found at Mr. Engarde's mansion would mean...

Japanese: 考えるまでもない！
English: It goes without saying, Your Honor.

Japanese: 当然、王都楼氏が 依頼主であることになる！
English: Mr. Matt Engarde is De Killer's client!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥静まりなさいッ！
English: Order! Order! Order! ...I said ORDER!!!

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥あなたにとって、 かなり不利な証拠でしたね。
English: Mr. Wright. This is a most unfortunate turn of events for you.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Yeah...

Japanese: ‥‥すみません、千尋さん。
English: Sorry, Mia.

Japanese: あなたの判断は まちがっていないわ。
English: No, it's alright. Your judgment was sound.

Japanese: 真実は、いつか カオを出すものよ。
English: Actually, I figured the bear would come up.

Japanese: ‥‥そのクマさん。
English: If not now, then it would have later on.

Japanese: あなたが提出しなくても、 きっと、あの検事さんが‥‥
English: Even if you hadn't shown it to the court, I'm sure your friend Edgeworth would've.

Japanese: （‥‥そうだな。あの場には アイツもいたんだから）
English: (Ah, I almost forgot that he knew about it too...)

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: どうやら、これ以上の審理は 必要ないようですね‥‥。
English: I think it is clear that there is no need for us to continue this trial.

Japanese: （こ‥‥このままじゃダメだ！ なんとかしないと‥‥）
English: (I... I can't let this happen! I have to do something...)

Japanese: （‥‥何か、もっともらしい ギモンを提示するんだ‥‥！）
English: (There has to be something we've overlooked!)

Japanese: 裁判長！　お待ちください！
English: Your Honor! A minute, please!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか？
English: Y-Yes, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 今までの審理で、まだ 不審な点が残されています！
English: There are still a few points left that we have not fully explored!

Japanese: なんだと‥‥！
English: What are you trying to pull!?

Japanese: ほ、ほお‥‥それは 聞き捨てなりませんな。
English: Oh... Well, we can't have that.

Japanese: アヤシイ点‥‥それは いったい、どこですか？
English: Alright, Mr. Wright. What questionable point would you like to explore further?

Japanese: それはモチロン、 荷星さんのケチな証言です！
English: Mr. Powers' testimony, of course!

Japanese: え！　た、たしかにボクはケチで
English: Huh? I know that my testimony was kind of shaky, but --

Japanese: 何を言うか、成歩堂！
English: Your inanity stupefies me, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 証言のアヤシイ点はすべて、 尋問中にただされているはずだ！
English: We have already clarified all questionable points during the cross-examination just now!
Urk.

Japanese: この期におよんで、その 証言にケチをつけるとは‥‥
English: Wasting time like this, calling the testimony questionable...

Japanese: ケチなのはキサマのほうだッ！
English: I'd say it's your head that's questionable here!

Japanese: ぐあああっ！
English: Aaah!

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりですな。
English: Yes, I agree.

Japanese: 尋問は正当に行われました。 証言に問題はありません。
English: The cross-examination went smoothly and there was nothing wrong with the testimony.

Japanese: それでは、これで‥‥
English: Now then, I believe --

Japanese: お‥‥お待ちください！
English: P-Please! Wait!

Japanese: なかなかシツコイですねえ あなたも。
English: You are being very persistent today.

Japanese: （このまま引き下がるワケには いかないんだ‥‥！）
English: (Because I can't let it end like this!)

Japanese: たしかに、今のぼくの 発言はアレでした。
English: I know my outburst just now was a little... questionable.

Japanese: アレだったな‥‥たしかに。
English: Questionable indeed.

Japanese: しかし！
English: But!

Japanese: アヤシイ点は、本当にまだ 残されているのです！
English: There really are some questionable points left to discuss, Your Honor!

Japanese: 荷星さんの証言で、 ハッキリしなかった点‥‥
English: There was one thing in Mr. Powers' testimony that was very unclear.

Japanese: それは、このアクセサリーを 受けとった人物です！
English: And that is the identity of the person who received the bear!

Japanese: そして、部屋の中のヒトに、 何やら手わたしたんですよ。
English: He gave something to the person inside the room.

Japanese: その。ザンネンながら、 ウデしか見えなかったんです。
English: I'm sorry, but I only saw the person's arm.

Japanese: 受けとった人物が、ハッキリ しない以上は‥‥、
English: As long as we don't know who it was that took the bear,

Japanese: その証言そのものが、その
English: we can't be sure of...

Japanese: あああああああッ！
English: Aaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: な、なんですか証人！
English: Wh-What is it, Mr. Powers!?

Japanese: 奇声を上げた以上は、 セキニンを持ちなさい！
English: If you're going to scream like that, then at least give us a good reason why!

Japanese: はあ‥‥きょ、恐縮です。
English: O-Oh, yeah... Sorry.

Japanese: ジツは‥‥その。 思い出したんですよ。
English: Actually... So... I remembered.

Japanese: あの、クマのアクセサリーを 受けとったヒトのコト‥‥
English: Um... I remembered who took the bear...
Wha--!?

Japanese: なんですとォ！
English: Really!?

Japanese: たしかにボク、ウデしか 見えなかったんですけど‥‥
English: I mean, I only saw his arm...

Japanese: で、でも、あれは‥‥
English: But... But... The arm...

Japanese: トノサマンでした！　あのウデ‥‥ ‥‥トノサマン・丙！
English: It was the Nickel Samurai's arm! I swear it!

Japanese: なんですって！
English: YOU'VE GOT TO BE KIDDING!!

Japanese: まちがいないかッ！
English: Are you sure of that, Mr. Powers!?

Japanese: ハイ！　あれはたしかに トノサマンでした！
English: Yeah! I'm sure it was the Nickel Samurai!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。 また墓穴をほりましたな。
English: It looks like you've dug your own grave... yet again.

Japanese: （今日、何個めだ？　これで‥‥）
English: (How many times is that today? ...I've lost count...)

Japanese: コイツを受けとったのは、 トノサマンだった‥‥
English: So the person who took in this little bear was the Nickel Samurai.

Japanese: トノサマン‥‥やはり、 王都楼　真悟ではないか！
English: And, as we all know, Matt Engarde is the Nickel Samurai!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人のおかげで、 さらにハッキリしましたな。
English: Thanks to the defense, we've made that all the clearer.

Japanese: どどど、どうしましょう！ 千尋さん！
English: What am I supposed to do now!? Mia, help!

Japanese: 迷っているヒマはないわ。
English: You don't have time to act lost.

Japanese: ベツの方面から疑問点を 探しましょう！　急いで！
English: You've got to find another angle to attack this from! Hurry!

Japanese: それでは、 この証人の尋問を‥‥
English: Now, I will bring this cross- examination to --

Japanese: 裁判長！　お待ちください！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: ま‥‥まだ何かあるんですか？
English: Again, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 今度こそ、ギモンの余地は 消え失せたではないですか。
English: We've already removed any and all questionable areas of this testimony.

Japanese: キサマもいっしょに 消え失せるんだな、成歩堂。
English: It's about time you were removed from this court, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （もう１つ‥‥探すんだ！ ギモン点を‥‥）
English: (I have to find something... Even one more little point will do!)

Japanese: ま、まだ‥‥ アヤシイ点は残されている！
English: There are... There are still questions left unanswered!

Japanese: アヤシイ点‥‥言うまでもなく、 このアクセサリーです！
English: I think it's fairly obvious that the bear itself is very questionable!

Japanese: そのクマが‥‥何か？
English: The bear... Mr. Wright?

Japanese: コイツは、王都楼さんの屋敷から 発見されました。
English: This was found at Mr. Engarde's mansion.

Japanese: しかし！　あの晩、王都楼さんは ホテルで逮捕されています！
English: However, Mr. Engarde was arrested at the hotel that night!

Japanese: つまり‥‥事件が発生してから、 彼は一度も家に帰っていない！
English: Which means that since the murder occurred, he has not had a chance to go home!
Oh...

Japanese: 結論はもう、おわかりでしょう。
English: I think Your Honor has already figured out what I'm trying to say.

Japanese: クマを屋敷に持ちこんだのは、 王都楼さんではあり得ないのです！
English: It is not possible that it was Mr. Engarde who took this bear to his mansion!

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに、その点は 見落としていました！
English: Wh-Why, that's very true! We didn't consider that point, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 被告人には、クマを持ち帰る 時間的よゆうはなかった‥‥
English: There was no way, timewise, for the defendant to have taken this bear home.

Japanese: （ふう‥‥これでなんとか、 ピンチは切り抜けたような
English: (Phew... Disaster averted it looks--

Japanese: ‥‥まだまだアマいな、成歩堂。
English: You haven't gotten the best of me yet, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと‥‥！
English: Huh!?

Japanese: 私は、ハッキリおぼえている。
English: I remember it clear as day.

Japanese: ‥‥王都楼の屋敷で キサマがつぶやいたコトバを‥‥。
English: I remember what you muttered to yourself at Engarde's mansion.

Japanese: このエリアは、完全に包囲した。 逃げることは不可能だ。
English: We have this area completely surrounded. There is no way for him to escape.

Japanese: まさか‥‥あの執事が 虎狼死家だったなんて‥‥
English: I can't believe it... That butler... All this time, he was De Killer...

Japanese: 虎狼死家と王都楼は、 いわば共犯関係にあった。
English: De Killer and Engarde were working together, so to speak.

Japanese: 彼は、王都楼の屋敷に ひそんでいたのだ‥‥執事として。
English: And De Killer was hiding at Engarde Mansion... as its butler.

Japanese: な‥‥なんとダイタンな‥‥
English: Wh-What a... bold move...

Japanese: クマのアクセサリーは、 虎狼死家が屋敷に持ち帰った。
English: The bear figurine was brought back to Engarde Mansion by De Killer himself.

Japanese: 逮捕されそうになったとき、 王都楼が彼にたのんだのだろう。
English: When it looked like he was about to be arrested, Engarde had him do so.

Japanese: ‥‥身体検査で発見されては マズいからな。
English: I assume because it would've been bad had the police found it during their investigation.
Hmm...

Japanese: どうかな？　弁護士クン。 さっきから、おとなしいが‥‥。
English: Well, Mr. Wright? You've been quiet for a while now...

Japanese: （ダメだ！　つけいるスキが まるでない‥‥）
English: (This is too much! Isn't there anything I can attack at all?)

Japanese: （もう１つ‥‥ベツの ギモン点を探すんだ！）
English: (I have to try... I have to find something else!)

Japanese: ‥‥どうですか？　弁護人。 今度こそ‥‥
English: What will you do now, Mr. Wright? Do you plan to...?

Japanese: 今度こそ、提示しましょう。 ‥‥ハッキリしたギモン点を！
English: I plan to expose a clearly shaky place in Mr. Powers' testimony!

Japanese: な！　ま、まだ何か あるんですか！
English: Wh-What!? There is still another one!?

Japanese: もう１つだけ！ アヤシイ点が残されています！
English: There is indeed, Your Honor. And it's quite a questionable point!

Japanese: ‥‥もう、けっこうです。
English: I think we've heard enough.

Japanese: クマのアクセサリーが 被告人の屋敷にあった理由も、
English: We now know why this bear figurine was at the defendant's mansion,

Japanese: 控え室で、殺し屋からそれを 受けとった人物も‥‥
English: as well as who it was that received the bear from the assassin in his room...

Japanese: すべてがハッキリしました。
English: Everything has become very clear.

Japanese: 殺害の依頼をしたのは、 王都楼　真悟だった！
English: The client who hired the assassin to commit the murder was Mr. Matt Engarde!

Japanese: これ以上の審理は必要ありません。 ‥‥判決を下します！
English: I see no reason for this trial to continue. Therefore, I will now hand down my verdict!

Japanese: ご理解をカンシャする‥‥
English: Thank you, Your Honor, for your understanding.

Japanese: 成歩堂。 ‥‥やはり‥‥
English: You see, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 真実には勝てなかったか。
English: You could not win against the truth, could you?

Japanese: （‥‥こうなることは わかりきっていた‥‥）
English: (I knew it would turn out this way...)

Japanese: （だって‥‥御剣の立証は まさに、真実なのだから‥‥）
English: (After all, what Edgeworth has stated is the truth...)

Japanese: よろしいですね？　弁護人。
English: Any last objections, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （‥‥どうなんだ？ “よろしい”のか‥‥？）
English: (Well, do I...? What should I do...?)

Japanese: 弁護側に‥‥ ‥‥異議は、ありま
English: The defense... rests--

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: 逃げる気？
English: Are you giving up?

Japanese: ‥‥あなたのヒトコトで‥‥ 真宵は死ぬのよ。
English: If you do... Maya will die.

Japanese: あなたはそれを、一生 背負っていくことになる。
English: And you'll carry that weight on your shoulders every day for the rest of your life.

Japanese: ‥‥そうはさせないわ。 ゼッタイに！
English: I don't want that to happen to you!

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥
English: Mia...

Japanese: 異議を申し立てなさい。
English: Raise an objection, Phoenix.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、判決を
English: I will now announce my ver--

Japanese: （審理を引きのばすための方法は、 もう１つしかない‥‥）
English: (There is only one way for me to drag this trial out...)

Japanese: （‥‥この、罪深い方法しか‥‥）
English: (The only thing I have left is this one dirty trick...)

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟が、暗殺の依頼人だと いう証拠は、以下の２点です。
English: Right now, we have these two reasons to believe my client is a client of the assassin.

Japanese: その１。殺し屋から、クマの アクセサリーを受けとった。
English: Reason number one. He accepted the bear figurine from the assassin.

Japanese: その２。アクセサリーが、 王都楼の屋敷から発見された。
English: Reason number two. That very same figurine was found at Engarde Mansion.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！
English: However!

Japanese: これらの証拠は、ある人物に よって仕組まれた可能性がある！
English: It's possible this is all the work of a certain other person!

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか‥‥？
English: What are you saying...?

Japanese: 殺人を虎狼死家に依頼した真犯人は ‥‥他にいるかもしれない！
English: What I am saying is, it's possible a different person is De Killer's real client!

Japanese: 今になって“真犯人”ですか‥‥
English: The "real" client...?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Yes.

Japanese: それがキサマの答えか。
English: Tsk, tsk. Is this all you have?

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、聞かせてください。
English: Now then, Mr. Wright. Let's hear your theory.

Japanese: あなたの言う“真犯人”‥‥ いったい、だれなのですか？
English: Who do you say is the real client of De Killer, and therefore, the real murderer?

Japanese: ‥‥私を失望させるな。
English: You've let me down, Mr. Wright.
Huh?

Japanese: キサマは真実を知っている。
English: I know you are aware of the truth.

Japanese: そこから目をそむけるのならば、 それでもよい。
English: And you are free to turn your eyes from it...

Japanese: しかし。せめて スジだけは通してくれ。
English: But at least try to make some sense while you are doing so.

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥もう一度、 考えなおしてもらいましょう。
English: I'll give you another chance. Don't squander it.

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒‥‥！
English: Adrian Andrews...!?

Japanese: 彼女は、王都楼に 罪を着せたことを認めています。
English: Yes. We already know that she tried to frame Matt Engarde for the crime...

Japanese: ‥‥２体目のトノサマンを使って！
English: By wearing a spare Nickel Samurai costume!

Japanese: あっ！
English: Ah!

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥ボクが見た、 あの“トノサマンのウデ”は‥‥
English: Then... Then the "Nickel Samurai's arm" that I saw...

Japanese: 霧緒さんだった可能性があります！
English: That could have very well been Ms. Andrews!

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥そのとき、 王都楼さん自身は‥‥？
English: But what about Mr. Engarde?

Japanese: 証言を思い出してください。
English: If you would please recall yesterday's testimony,

Japanese: 被告人は休憩時間中、 ヒルネをしていた！
English: the defendant was taking a nap during the break period.

Japanese: そ‥‥そういえば‥‥
English: That's right...

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥これが王都楼さんの 屋敷から見つかったのも‥‥？
English: Then... finding this figure at Mr. Engarde's mansion...?

Japanese: ‥‥華宮　霧緒が しかけた、ワナだったのです。
English: It was a well-laid trap set by Ms. Andrews.

Japanese: み‥‥御剣検事。 何か、ご意見は‥‥？
English: Mr. Edgeworth... What is your opinion on this?

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人の主張には あまりにムリが多すぎる。
English: I can't even begin to count the flaws in the defense's logic.

Japanese: そして、証拠のかけらもない。
English: Besides which, there is no evidence to support it.

Japanese: ただ‥‥可能性を完全に否定する ことはできない。
English: However... I can't fully discount its possibility either.

Japanese: ‥‥ふ‥‥ふむむむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥なんだよ、この裁判‥‥
English: ...What is with this trial...?

Japanese: ‥‥ダレが聞いたって、 真犯人は王都楼じゃないか‥‥
English: ...Come on. Anyone can tell Engarde did it...

Japanese: ‥‥ヒトに罪をなすりつけてまで、 依頼人を無罪にしたいのかしら‥‥
English: ...I can't believe the defense would go so far as to pin the guilt onto someone else...

Japanese: ‥‥なすりつける、って 殺人罪だぞ！　信じられねえよ‥‥
English: ...Yeah! Unbelievable! It's not something petty; it's murder of all things...!

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃんを 助けるためだ‥‥）
English: (This is to save Maya... This is to save Maya...)

Japanese: （たとえ世界中を敵に回しても、 戦いをやめるわけにはいかない）
English: (Even if the whole world turns against me, this is one fight I can't give up on!)

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ 従えない者には退廷を命じます！
English: Order! Order! Order! All disruptive parties will be forced to leave the courtroom!

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: “あるいは”と思ってはいた。 この弁護士が相手なら‥‥
English: For the benefit of the defense, I'm willing to play along with his "what if" game.

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか？
English: His "what if" game, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: 私には、弁護側の挑戦を 受ける用意がある‥‥
English: The prosecution is prepared to challenge the defense's theory.

Japanese: 成歩堂‥‥。 きみは、フシギに思わなかったか？
English: Mr. Wright. Even you must have thought it strange and wondered,

Japanese: 真犯人はなぜ、こんなモノを ほしがったのか‥‥
English: "Why would the criminal want this little wooden bear...?"

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥）
English: (He's right...)

Japanese: （虎狼死家はわざわざ、あのクマを 王都楼にとどけている‥‥）
English: (De Killer did specially bring that bear to Engarde right away...)

Japanese: 何か‥‥イミがあるのか？
English: Why do you ask? Is there something special about it?

Japanese: 当然だ。
English: Absolutely.

Japanese: そのイミを知れば‥‥ おのずとアキラカになるだろう。
English: And I'm sure that once the court knows its significance,

Japanese: ‥‥“だれが真犯人か”‥‥
English: the true killer's identity will become crystal clear.

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒の容疑を晴らす 証人を入廷させたい！
English: The prosecution calls upon a witness who will clear all doubts against Ms. Andrews.

Japanese: いったい‥‥だれですか？
English: And who would that be!?

Japanese: 言うまでもなく、彼女自身だ。 ‥‥華宮　霧緒。
English: It's quite simple, Your Honor. Ms. Adrian Andrews herself.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: I see...

Japanese: いったん、ここで １０分間の休憩をとりましょう。
English: Well then, the court will take a short 10 minute recess.

Japanese: 検察側は、証人の準備を おねがいします。
English: The prosecution will prepare its witness in that time.

Japanese: ‥‥了解した。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: 同日　午前１１時５４分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: March 23, 11:54 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: ‥‥ふっふっふっ‥‥
English: Hahaha...

Japanese: やっぱり、人質をとられると ちがうねぇ、弁護士さん‥‥。
English: Oh, I knew it was a good idea to hold her hostage. Don't you agree, Mister Lawyer...?

Japanese: まさか、あのキリオにオレの 罪を着せてくれるとは！
English: But I never thought in your desperation, you'd try to pin the guilt onto Adrian!

Japanese: （くそっ！　悪人め‥‥）
English: (Aaaargh! I swear this demon will pay...)

Japanese: なるほどくんっ！
English: Mr. Nick!

Japanese: あ、あれ‥‥春美ちゃん。 千尋さんは？
English: P-Pearls? Where's Mia?

Japanese: それが‥‥わからないのです。
English: I... don't know...

Japanese: 急に、強いチカラで 呼びもどされてしまって‥‥
English: A really strong power suddenly called her away...

Japanese: （強いチカラ‥‥？）
English: (A really strong power...?)

Japanese: あ、なるほどくん！ お電話が‥‥
English: Oh, Mr. Nick! Your phone is...

Japanese: （‥‥イトノコさんからだ！）
English: (It's from Gumshoe!)

Japanese: どッスか！　まだ、 持ちこたえているッスか！
English: How is it going!? Have you been hanging in there, pal!?

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥反則ギリギリで がんばってます。
English: Y-Yeah, sort of... We just barely found something to latch onto.

Japanese: ふう‥‥よかったッス‥‥
English: Phew. That's good, pal.

Japanese: それで‥‥！ そっちはどうなんですか？
English: And what about you!? Anything yet!?

Japanese: 虎狼死家と‥‥ 真宵ちゃんの行方は！
English: Have you figured out where De Killer and Maya are!?

Japanese: それが‥‥その。 さすがに手がかりゼロ、では‥‥
English: Um, uh... We still don't have any leads, but...

Japanese: そ、そんな！ もう、時間がありませんよ！
English: WHAT!? We don't have any more time!

Japanese: ‥‥何か１つ！ １つでも手がかりがあれば‥‥
English: If we just had one! Even a single clue would be really helpful...

Japanese: （‥‥“真宵ちゃんを救出するまで 持ちこたえる”‥‥）
English: (I was only able to come this far because I kept thinking to myself...)

Japanese: （それだけを考えて ここまで来たのに‥‥）
English: (I've got to keep the trial going until Maya's been rescued...)

Japanese: （もう‥‥今度こそ、 希望は断たれたのか‥‥？）
English: (But have I just run out of luck this time...? Is all our hope for naught...?)

Japanese: テントよ！
English: A tent!
(Huh? A tent!?)

Japanese: サーカスのテントが見えるわ！
English: I could see a circus tent!

Japanese: ち‥‥千尋さん！
English: M-Mia!

Japanese: あの子‥‥今まで 眠らされていたみたい。
English: It looks like Maya was unconscious until just a few minutes ago.

Japanese: 目がさめてすぐ、 私を呼びだしたの。
English: As soon as she woke up, she called for me.

Japanese: それで‥‥真宵ちゃんは！
English: So it was Maya that called you away!

Japanese: あの子は今、小さなホコリっぽい 部屋に閉じこめられている。
English: She's locked in a dusty little room right now.

Japanese: その窓からテントが見えたわ。 距離は‥‥約１００メートル！
English: But I could see a circus tent outside the window about 300 feet away!

Japanese: ‥‥イトノコさん！
English: Gumshoe!

Japanese: この街には今、いくつ サーカスのテントがありますか！
English: Is there a circus in town right now!?

Japanese: ‥‥１つきりッス。 タチミサーカスだけッス！
English: There's only one, pal! The Berry Big Circus!

Japanese: テントの周囲１００メートル付近！ そこに真宵ちゃんたちはいる！
English: Maya is somewhere within a 300 feet radius of the main tent!

Japanese: な‥‥なんスとォ！ ちょ、ちょっと待つッス！
English: Wh-What!? OK, hold on a sec, pal!

Japanese: おい！　地図に円を描け！ 急げ！　半径、１００メートルだ！
English: Hey! Draw a circle on that map! About a 300 feet radius from the main tent! Hurry!

Japanese: それから‥‥
English: And...

Japanese: それから？
English: And?

Japanese: 窓の下に、ポストが見えるわ。
English: I could see a mailbox under the window.

Japanese: イトノコさん！　ポストです！
English: Gumshoe! There's also a mailbox!

Japanese: ‥‥ムッ、了解！　他には！
English: Hmm... OK! What else?

Japanese: 他には！　千尋さん！
English: What else, Mia!?

Japanese: ごめんなさい。小さな窓だから 他には、何も‥‥。
English: I'm sorry, but it was a very small window. I couldn't see anything else...

Japanese: 窓は‥‥３階ほどの高さよ。 古いオフィスビルの一室ね‥‥
English: It felt like I was in an old office building... Maybe the third floor or so...

Japanese: ‥‥それでじゅうぶんッス！
English: I heard her! An old office building. Good stuff, pal!

Japanese: もう少しッス。がんばるッス！ これで、希望がつながったッス！
English: OK, just hang in there! Just a little longer, pal! Wish us luck!

Japanese: わかりました！
English: Good luck!

Japanese: また連絡するッス！ 電話の電源、切っちゃダメッスよ！
English: I'll call you later, so don't let your battery die, OK pal!?

Japanese: 千尋さん！　真宵ちゃんは‥‥ ブジですよね！
English: Mia! Maya's not hurt, right!?

Japanese: キケンな状況よ。かなり おなかをすかせているわ。
English: She's in a pretty bad state, Phoenix. She's being starved.

Japanese: （‥‥早く見つけてあげてくれ！ イトノコさん‥‥）
English: (Gumshoe! Please hurry!!)

Japanese: ‥‥そろそろ時間よ。 いいわね？　なるほどくん。
English: Looks like we're out of time. Are you alright, Phoenix?

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんが救出されるのは もう、時間の問題だ‥‥）
English: (It's only a matter of time before Maya's rescued...)

Japanese: （だいじょうぶ！ 持ちこたえてみせる！）
English: (I can do this! I just have to make this trial last a little longer!)

Japanese: 同日　午後１２時　５分 地方裁判所　第３法廷
English: March 23, 12:05 PM District Court Courtroom No. 3

Japanese: では、審理を再開しましょう。
English: Court will now reconvene.

Japanese: 被害者を殺害した虎狼死家は、 依頼人にコイツを手わたした。
English: De Killer, the man who murdered the victim, handed this to his client.

Japanese: そこで１つ、ギモンが生まれる。 “なぜ、こんなものを‥‥？”
English: From this, one obvious question arises. "Why this particular item?"

Japanese: その答えこそが、 真犯人の名前を語るだろう。
English: I believe the answer to that question will provide us with the name of the real criminal.

Japanese: ‥‥被告人のマネージャー、 華宮　霧緒を入廷させてもらおう！
English: Now then, the prosecution calls the defendant's manager, Adrian Andrews, to the stand!

Japanese: 証人は現在、死体損壊、 捜査妨害などの罪に問われている。
English: Currently, the witness is accused of tampering and obstruction of justice.

Japanese: しかし今日は、殺人の罪を 負うべき人物を探すため‥‥
English: However, you have been called to the witness stand today to ascertain...

Japanese: もう一度、証言台に 立っていただいた。
English: who exactly is guilty of murder.

Japanese: ‥‥わかっています。
English: I understand.

Japanese: では、さっそくうかがおう。 ‥‥このクマに見おぼえは？
English: Very good. Now, have you ever seen this bear before, Ms. Andrews?

Japanese: もちろん‥‥あります。
English: Of course I have.

Japanese: な、なんですと‥‥？
English: You have seen it before?

Japanese: そう。もちろん証人は 知っている。
English: That's right. It's only natural that the witness has.

Japanese: われわれに教えていただこう。 ‥‥このクマについて。
English: Ms. Andrews. Could you please enlighten the court to this bear's secrets?

Japanese: はい。
English: Alright.

Japanese: （き、霧緒さんが‥‥なぜ？）
English: (Why... Why does she...??)

Japanese: ～クマのアクセサリー～
English: -- The Bear Figurine --

Japanese: じつはそれ、精巧なパズルに なっているんです。
English: Actually, this is an elaborate puzzle.

Japanese: 正しい順序でパーツをいじると、 バラバラにすることができるの。
English: If you know the correct order, it can be taken apart one piece at a time.

Japanese: 中は空洞になっていて、小物を 入れることができるんです。
English: At its center is a small cavity, with just enough room to store a small item.

Japanese: かなり精巧だから‥‥やり方を 知らなければ、開けられません。
English: Because of its complexity, if you don't know the order, you can't open the bear.

Japanese: 見た目だけで“小物入れ”とは わかりませんしね。
English: You really can't tell that it's a small "jewelry box" just by looking at it.

Japanese: こ‥‥小物入れ‥‥なのですか？ このアクセサリーが‥‥
English: So this figurine... it's a container of sorts, is it...?

Japanese: 気がつきませんよね？ ‥‥見ただけでは。
English: Yes. Looks can be deceiving, wouldn't you agree?

Japanese: ええ。スバラシイ細工ですなあ。
English: Yes. This is superb craftsmanship.

Japanese: ‥‥あ。では、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: ...Oh, yes, I nearly forgot. You may begin your cross- examination, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: やはり、何かあったようね ‥‥あのクマさん‥‥。
English: It looks like there really was something to that bear after all.

Japanese: ～クマのアクセサリー～
English: -- The Bear Figurine --

Japanese: じつはそれ、精巧なパズルに なっているんです。
English: Actually, this is an elaborate puzzle.

Japanese: 正しい順序でパーツをいじると、 バラバラにすることができるの。
English: If you know the correct order, it can be taken apart one piece at a time.

Japanese: 中は空洞になっていて、小物を 入れることができるんです。
English: At its center is a small cavity, with just enough room to store a small item.

Japanese: かなり精巧だから‥‥やり方を 知らなければ、開けられません。
English: Because of its complexity, if you don't know the order, you can't open the bear.

Japanese: 見た目だけで“小物入れ”とは わかりませんしね。
English: You really can't tell that it's a small "jewelry box" just by looking at it.

Japanese: 私たちは、あのアクセサリーに ついて、何も知らないわ。
English: We don't know anything about this figurine.

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、情報を 集めましょう。
English: So we should try to find out more for now.

Japanese: そうですね。
English: Yeah, I'll keep pressing her for more information.

Japanese: パズル‥‥ですか？
English: A puzzle?

Japanese: そうです。
English: That's right.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥見た目は、 ふつうのアクセサリーですねえ。
English: Hmm... But it looks like an ordinary figurine...

Japanese: （たしかに。きっと、 何も知らない人間が見たら‥‥）
English: (True enough...)

Japanese: （パズルだとは わからないだろうな）
English: (To people who don't know, I'm sure they would never guess that this was a puzzle...)

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、どういう パズルなんですか？
English: So what kind of puzzle is this exactly?

Japanese: “パーツをいじる”‥‥ どんなふうにやるんですか？
English: So you can "take it apart"? And how would one go about doing that?

Japanese: まず、シッポを右に回すと、 押しこめるようになるんです。
English: Well, you first turn its tail to the right and then push it in.

Japanese: ‥‥お。ホントですね。
English: ...Oh, yes, I see.

Japanese: すると、手や足やアタマの パーツが動くようになります。
English: After that, the arms and legs are free to move and can be removed.

Japanese: ほほ。これはおもしろい。
English: Ooh... This is most interesting...

Japanese: （裁判長、ムチュウに なってるぞ‥‥）
English: (A boy and his new toy... It's like he's 5 all over again...)

Japanese: ‥‥あ。私にはかまわず、 進めておいてください。
English: ...Oh, don't mind me. Go ahead and carry on.

Japanese: （いいのか‥‥？）
English: (I think he's lost it...)

Japanese: そのパズルを解くと‥‥ どうなるのだろうか？
English: So what do you find after you take the puzzle apart?

Japanese: どうして‥‥あなたが そんなことを知っているんですか？
English: And how do you know about this...?

Japanese: カンタンなコトです。 ‥‥私が買ってきたから。
English: I know because I was the one who bought it.

Japanese: なんですって！
English: Huh!?

Japanese: 友人とスイスへ旅行に行った おみやげにね。
English: It was a souvenir from when a friend and I went to Switzerland.

Japanese: じゃあ、これは‥‥あなたからの プレゼントだったのですか！
English: Then, this... this was a present from you!?

Japanese: そうです。
English: That's right.

Japanese: クマのカタチをしていて、 あの人にピッタリだと思って。
English: It was a puzzle in the shape of a bear, so I thought it would be perfect for Juan.

Japanese: （霧緒さんのプレゼント‥‥）
English: (So it was a present from Ms. Andrews...)

Japanese: ‥‥証人。 証言をつづけてもらいたい。
English: Witness, let's continue with your testimony.

Japanese: そのパズルの解き方を 知っていたのは？
English: So who exactly knew how to solve this puzzle?

Japanese: ‥‥２人だけです。 藤見野　イサオと、私。
English: Only the two of us, Juan and myself.

Japanese: スイスのおみやげだから、 これと同じものを持ってる人は‥‥
English: It was a souvenir from Switzerland...

Japanese: この国にはあまり、 いないでしょうね。
English: So I doubt there are that many people with this same bear in this country.

Japanese: でも、これだったら すぐコワせそうですね。
English: But this looks like it can be easily broken.

Japanese: ‥‥中のものを 取り出すだけでしたら。
English: Especially if someone wanted to get what was inside.

Japanese: オモチャですからね。
English: Well, it's a toy.

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、コワしてしまったら、 もとにはもどせませんけど。
English: But it can never be the same again once it's been broken.

Japanese: それが“小物入れ”だと 知っていた人物は？
English: Who else knows that this bear is actually a small container, or "jewelry box"?

Japanese: 私はだれにも しゃべっていません。
English: I never told anyone.

Japanese: 藤見野も話していなければ、 知っているのは２人きり‥‥。
English: And as long as Juan never told anyone either, then only the two of us knew...

Japanese: ２人きり、ですか‥‥。
English: The two of you, huh...

Japanese: では当然、王都楼さんも 知らないわけですね？
English: Then of course that means Mr. Engarde didn't know, right?

Japanese: （とりあえず‥‥このへんで 情報をまとめておこう）
English: (I think this is about all I'm going to get for now...)

Japanese: 証拠品《アクセサリー》の データを書きなおした。
English: Figurine updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥さて。
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: そろそろ気がすんだかな？ ‥‥弁護人。
English: I think even you have come to realize...

Japanese: この尋問で、おさえるべき ポイントは、たった１つだ。
English: That there is one very important fact we have uncovered, and that is this:

Japanese: すなわち。 “コイツは小物入れだった”
English: This bear is actually a "jewelry box".

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: われわれはすでに、 そのポイントをおさえた。
English: Now that we have agreed to this point,

Japanese: それならば‥‥次に 知るべきことも、たった１つ。
English: there is only one logical question that can come next. And that is this:

Japanese: すなわち。 “小物入れの中身は何か？”
English: What is inside this box?

Japanese: な‥‥何か入っているのですか！
English: What's inside!?

Japanese: それを、これからたしかめる。 ‥‥証人。
English: That's right. That's what we are going to find out next. Witness.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes?

Japanese: これを開けられるのは あなただけだ。
English: You are the only one who can open this.

Japanese: ‥‥おねがいする。
English: Please...

Japanese: （霧緒さんが パズルを解くあいだ‥‥）
English: (There's a painful silence hanging over the courtroom...)

Japanese: （法廷内は、いたいほどの 沈黙に支配された‥‥）
English: (All eyes are on Ms. Andrews now as she solves the puzzle, and takes the bear apart...)

Japanese: ‥‥解けました。
English: I've opened it.

Japanese: これ‥‥で よろしいかしら？
English: Is this what you wanted?

Japanese: な、なんですか？　それ。 ‥‥メモ‥‥みたいですが‥‥
English: Wh-What is that? It looks like a... note...

Japanese: 聞くまでもない。 ‥‥そうだな？　弁護人。
English: I don't think we need to guess at what that is... Do we, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 遺書だ。
English: It's the suicide note.

Japanese: いしょ‥‥
English: The suicide note?

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオのマネージャー、 天野　由利恵が残したものだ。
English: The suicide note left by Juan Corrida's former manager, Celeste Inpax.

Japanese: たった今まで行方不明だったが ‥‥やはり、かくされていたのだ。
English: Until now, no one knew of its whereabouts... But just as we suspected, it was hidden.

Japanese: 死体の発見者、 藤見野　イサオによって。
English: Hidden by the victim, Juan Corrida himself.

Japanese: ‥‥なかなかキレイな 字を書く女性だったようだな。
English: It seems Celeste Inpax had very beautiful handwriting.

Japanese: “天野　由利恵”‥‥このとおり、 本人のサインも入っている。
English: And she just as beautifully signed her own name on this document.

Japanese: これこそが、自殺の際に 彼女が残した遺書なのだ！
English: This is most definitely the note she left right before she committed suicide!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！
English: O-ORDER!

Japanese: 証人！　あなたは、 このことを‥‥？
English: Witness! Did you know about this...?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。藤見野から聞いて 知ってました。
English: ...Yes, I did. I heard all about it from Juan.

Japanese: 死体を発見したとき‥‥ 私、アクセサリーを探しました。
English: When I discovered his body... I looked for the bear.

Japanese: ‥‥公表される前に 始末してしまいたかったから。
English: I wanted to destroy the note before it became public.

Japanese: でも‥‥見つからなかった。
English: But... I couldn't find it anywhere.

Japanese: すでに、持ち去られていたのだ。 ‥‥虎狼死家の手によって。
English: Because it had already been taken by De Killer.

Japanese: ‥‥完全に、検事さんのペースね。
English: Everything is going at Mr. Edgeworth's pace...

Japanese: 遺書が発見された。 ‥‥じゃあ、次に問題になるのは？
English: So now that the suicide note has been found, what's the next logical question?

Japanese: “何が書いてあるか‥‥？”
English: "What is written on the note...?"

Japanese: ‥‥そう。 まあ、想像はつくけど‥‥
English: That's right... At least, that's what I would think...

Japanese: それではここで、遺書の内容を 公表したいと思う。
English: Now then, I believe it is only appropriate the contents of this note be made known.

Japanese: ‥‥もう、どうしようも ないんでしょうね‥‥。
English: I can't stop you, can I...?

Japanese: それを手に入れるために ずいぶんムダなことをしたのに。
English: I went through so much... just to get my hands on it.

Japanese: 彼女のためにも‥‥ 燃やしてしまいたかった。
English: And I was going to burn it... for her sake.

Japanese: ‥‥申しわけないが、 それはできない。
English: I'm deeply sorry, but I can't allow you to persuade me to stop.

Japanese: 裁判長。遺書の内容を 読み上げていただきたい。
English: Your Honor. If you could please read the contents of this note aloud.

Japanese: わかりました‥‥
English: Very well...

Japanese: 静まりかえった法廷内に、 裁判長の声だけがひびく‥‥。
English: The judge's voice rang loud and clear though the dead silent courtroom...

Japanese: ‥‥由利恵さんは、 すべて書き記していた。
English: In her note, Celeste Inpax left to us a record of all that had happened to her...

Japanese: 王都楼に、いいように 利用され、捨てられて‥‥
English: About being used and then thrown away by Engarde...

Japanese: いざ結婚が決まると、 今度はそれをジャマされて‥‥
English: About being engaged to Corrida, and Engarde's role in destroying that...

Japanese: 絶望のうちに 死を決心したこと‥‥
