English: And about how she decided, in her despair, to end it all.

Japanese: ‥‥以上です。
English: And that's all Ms. Inpax had to say.

Japanese: ‥‥１つだけ言っておきたい。
English: There is one thing I would like to say here.

Japanese: 検察側は、王都楼　真悟の 人格を攻撃するつもりはない。
English: The prosecution has no interest in slandering Mr. Engarde.

Japanese: では、いったい‥‥？
English: Then... What...?

Japanese: もちろん、その目的は、 殺人の動機を立証することにある。
English: Our intention, Your Honor, is to establish a motive for murder.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだな？　証人。
English: Isn't that correct, witness?

Japanese: ええ。
English: Yes...

Japanese: 事件があった晩、藤見野は その遺書を公表するつもりでした。
English: On the night of the murder, Juan was going to make the contents of the note public.

Japanese: 授賞式のあと‥‥ 記者会見を開いて。
English: After the post-ceremony show... he was going to hold a press conference.

Japanese: なんですって‥‥。
English: My word...

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟は、何よりその サワヤカなイメージが大切だった。
English: Matt Engarde values above all else, his "refreshing like a spring breeze" image.

Japanese: だから‥‥遺書の公表だけは、 阻止しなければならなかったのだ。
English: Which is why he had to stop this note from being made public...

Japanese: いかなる手段を使っても！
English: At any cost!

Japanese: 証拠品《由利恵の遺書》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Celeste's Suicide Note added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥自分のせいで人を 自殺に追いこんでおいて‥‥
English: ...It's Engarde's fault that woman killed herself...

Japanese: ‥‥今度は、それをバラそうとした 人間まで、ダマらせたんだよ！
English: ...And this time, he even went so far as to kill someone to stop him from revealing that!

Japanese: ‥‥ひどい‥‥！ なんて、自分勝手な人なの‥‥
English: ...How terrible...! What a selfish person...!

Japanese: ‥‥そんなクズを、すずしいカオで 弁護してるヤツもいるケドな！
English: I guess there are slimeball lawyers out there who will defend these creeps too!

Japanese: 今度こそ、ギモンの余地はない。
English: There is no margin for doubt here.

Japanese: 虎狼死家に依頼した人物の目的は、 この遺書にあった。
English: Mr. de Killer's client's goal was to obtain this suicide note.

Japanese: そして、その遺書がどうしても 必要だったのは‥‥被告人だ。
English: And the only person who needed this note that badly is the defendant.

Japanese: そしてジッサイ、遺書は 被告の屋敷から見つかった！
English: Let's not forget that the bear with the note inside was found at the defendant's house!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、私たちは 真相にたどりついたようですね。
English: It seems that we have come to the truth at last.

Japanese: 被告の犯行動機は自己中心的で、 同情の余地は、まったくない！
English: The defendant's motives were entirely selfish. He deserves no sympathy from anyone!

Japanese: ううう‥‥ （もう、逃げようがないぞ！）
English: Urk... (How am I supposed to escape from this one!?)

Japanese: ‥‥どうしたの？ なるほどくん。
English: Why the hesitation, Phoenix?

Japanese: まだ、あなたの電話は 鳴っていないわ。
English: Gumshoe hasn't called yet, so you know what you must do.

Japanese: （これでもまだ‥‥食いさがって いかなければならない！）
English: (I know. I have to carry on and buy him some more time!)

Japanese: （致命的な証拠は２つ。 アクセサリーと、あの遺書だ）
English: (OK. There are 2 deadly pieces of evidence: the figurine and the suicide note.)

Japanese: （そのどちらかに‥‥逃げ道が あるのかもしれない！）
English: (Maybe somehow, I can find a way out of this situation through one of those!)

Japanese: 裁判長が木槌を手に取ったわ。 ‥‥早く！
English: The gavel is already in the judge's hand! Phoenix! Hurry!

Japanese: （遺書か！　アクセサリーか！ ‥‥アヤシイのはどっちだ‥‥）
English: (The suicide note or the figurine!? Which one of these should I pursue??)

Japanese: お待ちください、裁判長！
English: Please wait, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥見ろよ、あの弁護士‥‥
English: ...Oh man, look at that lawyer...

Japanese: ‥‥まだ認めない気なの‥‥？
English: ...He's still going at it...?

Japanese: ‥‥犯人を無罪にしようなんて、 とんでもない弁護士ね！
English: ...It's like he doesn't care that he's trying to get a killer off the hook!

Japanese: ‥‥この遺書を取りもどすため、 王都楼　真悟は殺害を決意した。
English: I think Your Honor believes that Matt Engarde killed in order to obtain this note.

Japanese: そのとおりです。
English: Yes, that is correct.

Japanese: しかし‥‥それはおかしい。
English: But that seems a little strange.

Japanese: ムジュンしています！
English: In fact, I think there is a contradiction here!

Japanese: この遺書は、藤見野　イサオが 事件当夜まで、かくしていた。
English: This note was hidden by Mr. Corrida until the night of the murder.

Japanese: それならば！
English: If that is the case...

Japanese: その遺書の内容を、 王都楼　真悟が知るはずがない！
English: I say that Matt Engarde could not have known what was written on this note!

Japanese: あ‥‥！ そ、それはたしかに‥‥
English: Oh! I didn't think of it that way...

Japanese: ‥‥そう。最初から、 考え方がおかしかったのです。
English: Exactly. But I did think of it that way, and I thought it was rather strange.

Japanese: 内容も知らない遺書のために、 殺害を計画するはずがありません！
English: No one in their right mind would kill for a note without first knowing what it said!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛にッ！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: お待ちください、裁判長！
English: Please wait, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥見ろよ、あの弁護士‥‥
English: ...Oh man, look at that lawyer...

Japanese: ‥‥まだ認めない気なの‥‥？
English: ...He's still going at it...?

Japanese: ‥‥犯人を無罪にしようなんて、 とんでもない弁護士ね！
English: ...It's like he doesn't care that he's trying to get a killer off the hook!

Japanese: 殺し屋は、イサオさんを殺害後、 コイツを現場から持ち去った。
English: The assassin took this with him from the crime scene after murdering Mr. Corrida.

Japanese: ‥‥依頼人にたのまれたからです。
English: At the request of his client, of course.

Japanese: それがどうかしましたか？
English: So, what's your point, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 依頼人が王都楼　真悟だとすると、 こんなことはあり得ないのです。
English: I don't think it's possible that Mr. de Killer's client was Matt Engarde.

Japanese: ムジュンしています！
English: In fact, I think there is a contradiction here!

Japanese: このクマは、見た目から “小物入れ”とはわかりません。
English: You can't tell by just looking at it that this bear is really a jewelry box.

Japanese: まして、この中に遺書が 入っているなんて‥‥
English: The chances that Matt Engarde thought the note was inside this bear...

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟が、それを 知っているはずがない！
English: Are zero to none!

Japanese: あ‥‥！ そ、それはたしかに‥‥
English: Oh! I didn't think of it that way...

Japanese: ‥‥そう。最初から、 考え方がおかしかったのです。
English: Exactly. But I did think of it that way, and I thought it was rather strange.

Japanese: 王都楼さんが、このアクセサリーを ほしがるハズがないのです！
English: After all, there is no reason why Mr. Engarde would ever want a jewelry box like this!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛にッ！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: た‥‥たしかにそのとおりです！
English: Y-You make a valid point, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: い、いかがですか！　御剣検事‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth! What is your opinion!?

Japanese: （‥‥とっさの思いつきに しては、うまくいったな‥‥）
English: (It was just a flash, but I think I did rather well on this one...)

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンながら。
English: Unfortunately...

Japanese: 彼は、そう思っていないようね。
English: I think he believes differently.

Japanese: ‥‥礼を言わせていただこう。
English: I believe a show of appreciation is in order.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか？
English: H-Huh?

Japanese: 弁護側は、さらなる絶望を 望んでいるようだ。
English: The defense seems to be in love with wishing more despair upon itself.

Japanese: ‥‥これを見ていただきたい。
English: I would like to direct the court's attention to this.

Japanese: それは‥‥なんですか？
English: What is that...?

Japanese: 超小型のビデオカメラだ。
English: It is a very small video camera, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥その性能ゆえ、盗撮目的に 使用されることも多い。
English: This type of camera is commonly used as a means of spying.

Japanese: と、とうさつ‥‥
English: S-Spying...?
(What the...!?)

Japanese: （あの盗撮カメラなら、 ぼくが持ってるのに‥‥）
English: (I thought that spy camera was in my possession...)

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟と被害者は、たがいに 異常なライバル意識を抱いていた。
English: Matt Engarde and the victim both thought of the other as their biggest rival.

Japanese: ついには、こんなものを使って、 相手の弱みをにぎろうとしたのだ！
English: They even went so far as to use this type of item to find each other's weaknesses!

Japanese: つ、つまり‥‥？
English: And...?

Japanese: 被害者の藤見野　イサオは、 私生活を監視されていた！
English: The victim, Juan Corrida, was being spied on!

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、 王都楼　真悟によって！
English: His personal life was being watched by none other than Matt Engarde!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛にぃぃッ！ コラ、弁護人ッ！
English: Order! Oooooorder!! Ahem! Mr. Wright!

Japanese: は‥‥はい。
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: まさか、あなたは‥‥ 盗撮の事実を知っていたのですか！
English: You... Don't tell me you knew about your client's spying activities!?

Japanese: え、ええ‥‥まあ。
English: Well... sort of...

Japanese: “まあ”じゃありませんッ！
English: "Sort of" is not an acceptable answer, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥おそらくキミは、 フシギに思っていることだろう。
English: I see you are confused, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: “クマの目にしかけられたカメラは ぼくが持ってるのに”‥‥と。
English: You're probably thinking, "But I have the camera that was in the stuffed bear's eye..."

Japanese: 私の持っているこのカメラは、 それとはベツのものだ。
English: But this camera that I have is not that same one.

Japanese: ‥‥ゆうべ、ちょっと思いついて 被害者の自宅を調べさせた。
English: Last night, I searched the victim's house on a hunch...

Japanese: ‥‥コイツを使って。
English: Using this...

Japanese: （イトノコさんの探知機‥‥）
English: (Gumshoe's bug sweeper...)

Japanese: ちなみに、このカメラには 被告人の指紋が残されていた。
English: By the way, Mr. Wright. The defendant's fingerprints were found on this camera.

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟の‥‥ 指紋ですってッ！
English: Matt Engarde's fingerprints were on there!?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: Well, Phoenix...

Japanese: カメラは、いろいろなところに しかけられていたようね。
English: It looks like those cameras were hidden all over the place, huh?

Japanese: （なんてこった‥‥）
English: (What am I supposed to say to that evidence...?)

Japanese: ‥‥これでおわかりだろう。
English: I think this is the end.

Japanese: 王都楼が遺書の情報を つかんだのは、当然のことなのだ。
English: It's fairly obvious that Mr. Engarde learned of the suicide note through this.

Japanese: ‥‥こうして、被害者を 見張っていたのだから‥‥
English: He was watching the victim all along.

Japanese: （カンゼンに‥‥やられた‥‥！）
English: (He got me good this time! I don't have anything to counter that!)

Japanese: ‥‥おいおい、まだ何か 考えてるぞ、あの弁護士‥‥
English: ...Hey, hey... Now what's that lawyer thinking...?

Japanese: ‥‥ママー。あのおにいちゃん、 サツジンハンなの？
English: ...Mommy? Is that man the bad killer guy?

Japanese: ‥‥シッ！　ダメよ！ 目を合わせちゃ！
English: ...Shush! Stop! Don't look at him!

Japanese: ‥‥あの冷や汗、キモチワルイ‥‥
English: ...The way he's sweating is just so... Eww... Nasty...

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥
English: Phoenix...

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Yes, Chief!?

Japanese: どうかしら。 次の手は見つかった？
English: Have you figured out what you're going to do next yet?

Japanese: 次の手‥‥！ （そんなもの、あるわけが‥‥）
English: What I'm going to do next!? (Does running away like a frightened child work?)

Japanese: たしかに、御剣検事の立証は カンペキに近いわ。
English: I know it seems like Mr. Edgeworth is very close to putting the lid on this case.

Japanese: ‥‥でも。
English: But...

Japanese: 立証を急ぐあまり、彼は１つ 重大な見落としをしているわ。
English: In his eagerness to prove his point, he forgot one very important thing.

Japanese: なんですって‥‥！
English: Well, what is it, Mia!?

Japanese: 当然するべき“検査”を していない証拠品がある。
English: There is a piece of evidence that he really should investigate.

Japanese: （当然するべき“検査”‥‥？）
English: (Something he should investigate?)

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、その“検査”をする チャンスは存在しなかったから‥‥
English: I would really hate to see the good prosecutor get scolded...

Japanese: 検事さんを責めるのも カワイソウだけど、ね。
English: For not remembering to look into the item when he had the chance.

Japanese: （どういうことだ‥‥？）
English: (Why are you speaking in riddles all of a sudden...?)

Japanese: ‥‥今度こそ‥‥ すべてがハッキリしました。
English: Alright, I think this time we finally understand everything.

Japanese: どうですか？　弁護人。 もう、異議はないでしょうな？
English: Well, Mr. Wright? You don't have any further objections, do you?

Japanese: （千尋さんの言う “不完全な証拠品”‥‥）
English: (What is this piece of evidence that Mia is talking about...?)

Japanese: （このぼくに 指摘できるのか‥‥？）
English: (Can I figure out what it is that still needs to be looked at, or should I let it go...?)

Japanese: （王都楼が有罪‥‥ 異議なんて、あるはずがない）
English: (I have no objections to Engarde getting his guilty sentence as he should...)

Japanese: （しかし‥‥今はまだ、 それを認めるときではない！）
English: (But I can't let that happen yet!)

Japanese: （千尋さんは言った。 “不完全な証拠品がある”と）
English: (Mia said there's still something worth examining.)

Japanese: （ならば‥‥ムリヤリでも、 そいつを提示するしかない！）
English: (If she thinks so, then even if I have to force it, I must find something to bring up!)

Japanese: ‥‥異議あり。
English: ...I have an objection, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。 ココロなしか、声が小さいな。
English: Hmph. That was about the weakest objection I've ever heard, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 裁判長ッ！　弁護側はまだ、 ナットクしたわけではありません！
English: Your Honor! The defense has no intentions of letting this go so easily!

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、ヤケになって いませんか‥‥？
English: You are beginning to sound desperate...

Japanese: 気のせいです！
English: That's just your imagination, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥御剣。きみらしくもないな。
English: Mr. Edgeworth. This is not like you at all.

Japanese: 立証を急ぐあまり‥‥ １つ、重大な見落としをしている。
English: In your eagerness to prove your point, you've forgotten one very important thing.

Japanese: それ‥‥私のセリフじゃない。
English: Hey... Isn't that what I just said!?

Japanese: ‥‥この私が 重大な見落とし‥‥だと？
English: So, you're telling me that I forgot something?

Japanese: おしかったな、御剣‥‥
English: You're so close, Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: 当然するべき“検査”を、きみは できなかった。‥‥この証拠品に！
English: But there's something you really should examine about this piece of evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥い、いかがですか？ 御剣検事‥‥。
English: Well, Mr. Edgeworth...?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥“検査”が 必要だったかもしれない。
English: Yes, I do believe some special examination is needed...

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人のノーミソに。
English: But I think the item that should be examined is the defense's grey matter.

Japanese: え。
English: Whoops!

Japanese: 弁護人。法廷が終了したら、 私のところに来てください。
English: Mr. Wright. After this trial is over, I would like to see you in my chambers.

Japanese: ‥‥じっくり語り合いましょう。
English: I think there are a few things we need to discuss.

Japanese: はあ‥‥
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor...

Japanese: フッ‥‥
English: Hmph.

Japanese: 言いがかりをつけるなら、 もう少しアタマを使いたまえ。
English: If you're going to speak, at least use your brain to make up something intelligible.

Japanese: それは‥‥ 由利恵さんの遺書、ですか‥‥
English: That is... Ms. Inpax's suicide note, right?

Japanese: さあ‥‥。 それはどうでしょうか。
English: Hmm... Who knows?

Japanese: たしかに、“遺書”は この中から発見されました。
English: I mean, sure, this "suicide note" was found inside this bear.

Japanese: そして、ついさっきまで、 このぼくが持っていた。
English: But this bear was in my possession until a few moments ago.

Japanese: ということは‥‥、
English: Which means...

Japanese: その“遺書”は当然、まだ 筆跡の鑑定がされていない！
English: The handwriting on this "suicide note" has yet to be analyzed!
Oh...

Japanese: つまり！
English: So!

Japanese: 由利恵さんが、本当にこれを 書いたかどうか‥‥
English: As to whether this pivotal piece of evidence was really written by Ms. Inpax or not...

Japanese: もっとも重要な立証が マッタク、できていない！
English: Has yet to be even remotely confirmed!

Japanese: ‥‥べ、弁護人！ あなたはまさか‥‥
English: M-Mr. Wright! You can't seriously be suggesting...

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: あなた‥‥この遺書が ニセモノだって言うつもりなの！
English: You... Are you saying this suicide note is a fake!?

Japanese: ‥‥霧緒さん。
English: Ms. Andrews...

Japanese: あなたは、王都楼さんに殺人の 罪を着せようとしていた。
English: You were the one who tried to pin this murder on Mr. Engarde.

Japanese: 自分でニセの遺書を作って、 この中に入れたのかもしれない！
English: Who's to say you didn't create a fake "suicide note" and put it into this bear!?

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: H-How dare you!?

Japanese: 弁護人はまた、証拠もナシに 証人を犯人呼ばわりしている！
English: Your Honor. The defense is indiscriminately accusing the witness again!

Japanese: この証人と遺書を結びつける 証拠など、ないではないか！
English: There is no evidence linking the witness to the suicide note whatsoever!

Japanese: もしコイツがニセモノならば！ それを作れたのは証人しかいない！
English: But if this is a fake, then the witness is the only person who could have made it!

Japanese: どういうことだッ！
English: What!?

Japanese: このアクセサリーに関する 証言を思い出してください！
English: Recall the witness' testimony concerning this figurine!

Japanese: パズルを解くことができたのは、 被害者以外に、証人しかいない！
English: The only person other than the victim who could solve the puzzle is the witness herself!

Japanese: あッ‥‥！
English: Ahh!

Japanese: 霧緒さん！
English: Ms. Andrews!

Japanese: コイツは、あなたが 書いたんじゃないんですか？
English: You wrote this note, didn't you?

Japanese: 王都楼さんに罪を着せるために！
English: You wrote it so you could use it to frame Matt Engarde!

Japanese: そんな‥‥ I...
わ‥‥私、そんなコト‥‥ やっていません！
English: I did no such thing!

Japanese: 成歩堂！　この遺書が ニセモノだというのなら‥‥
English: Wright! If you're going to pronounce this suicide note a fake...

Japanese: その証拠を見せてみろッ！
English: Then show this court some evidence to support your theory!

Japanese: 御剣！　この紙キレを証拠として 提出したのは、お前のほうだ！
English: Mr. Edgeworth! You were the one who presented this scrap of paper as evidence!

Japanese: コイツがホンモノであることを 示すのは、検察側のシゴトだッ！
English: That means the burden of proof lies with you, the prosecution!

Japanese: ‥‥グゥゥゥッ！
English: Uuugn!

Japanese: そこまでッ！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事。この遺書の 筆跡鑑定は‥‥？
English: Mr. Edgeworth. Can you confirm the handwriting on this suicide note...?

Japanese: お聞きのとおりだ！ できるわけがないではないか！
English: It is as the defense has stated! The handwriting has yet to be analyzed!

Japanese: そうなると‥‥
English: If that's the case...

Japanese: どうやら、また判決は 下せないことになりますな。
English: It seems that yet again we have reached a point where a verdict is impossible.
Imposs...

Japanese: そんなバカなッ！
English: That's impossible!

Japanese: ‥‥マズいわね、なるほどくん。
English: This isn't good, Phoenix.

Japanese: （あと１日、決着が のびてしまったら‥‥）
English: (The judge is going to carry this trial over one more day.)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんの身体が‥‥ もう、もたない！）
English: (I don't think Maya will physically be able to make it another day!)

Japanese: ‥‥やむを得ませんな。
English: I didn't want to have to do this, but I don't have a choice.

Japanese: 検察側、弁護側の両者とも、 もう一度、調査を‥‥
English: I request that both the prosecution and defense further investigate...

Japanese: ‥‥筆跡鑑定なんて、弁護士の 時間かせぎに決まってるよ！
English: ...Handwriting analysis my butt! That's just the lawyer trying to buy more time!

Japanese: ‥‥王都楼は有罪よ！ だれだってわかるじゃない！
English: ...Engarde is guilty! Look, any idiot can tell you that!

Japanese: ‥‥もう‥‥ 限界、なのかもね‥‥
English: I think we've reached the end of the line...

Japanese: ‥‥有罪！　有罪！　有罪！‥‥ ‥‥有罪！　有罪！　有罪！‥‥
English: ...Guilty! Guilty! Guilty!... ...Guilty! Guilty! Guilty!... ...Guilty! Guilty! Guilty!...

Japanese: な‥‥なんですかこれは？
English: Wh-What is that sound!?

Japanese: （‥‥イトノコさんだ‥‥！）
English: (It's Gumshoe!)

Japanese: もしもし！　イトノコさん！
English: Hello!? Gumshoe!?
*sigh*...

Japanese: （なな、なんだ！　この、 いきなりのタメイキは！）
English: (What is with him!? And what's with that sigh!?)

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんは！ ‥‥犯人はどうなりましたか！
English: Where's Maya!? What happened to De Killer!?

Japanese: それが、そのお‥‥
English: He, uh...

Japanese: ‥‥逃げられたッス。
English: He got away...

Japanese: なんですって！
English: WHAT!?

Japanese: ごめんッス！　すまねッス！ 合わせるカオがないッス！
English: I'm sorry, pal! I really am! I don't know what to say besides I'm sorry.

Japanese: できるコトなら、ソーメンで クビつって、死んじまいたいッス！
English: I wish there was some way to make it up to you! I really do!

Japanese: と、とにかく‥‥状況を！ 聞かせてください、早く！
English: A-Anyway, what's going on!?

Japanese: たった今、アジトを見つけたッス。
English: We found his hideout, pal.

Japanese: でも‥‥もう、すでに ２人とも、いなかったッス‥‥
English: But... the two of them were already gone...

Japanese: ‥‥サイアクの事態ね‥‥
English: ...This is terrible...

Japanese: じ、自分はまだやれるッス！ もう少し、時間を‥‥
English: I'm going to keep looking for them, pal. Don't you worry! I just need a little more time!
But...

Japanese: ‥‥もしかして‥‥ もう、限界‥‥ッスか？
English: Don't tell me we don't... we don't have any more...

Japanese: ‥‥有罪！　有罪！　有罪！‥‥ ‥‥有罪！　有罪！　有罪！‥‥
English: ...Guilty! Guilty! Guilty!... ...Guilty! Guilty! Guilty!... ...Guilty! Guilty! Guilty!...

Japanese: 聞こえませんか？ ‥‥この、有罪コール‥‥
English: Do you hear that? They're calling for his head!

Japanese: ‥‥すまねッス‥‥。 ここまでやっておきながら‥‥
English: Mr. Wright... I can't... For us to come this far and...

Japanese: そッス！
English: Oh!

Japanese: な、なんですか！
English: Wh-What is it!?

Japanese: 御剣検事にかわってほしいッス！
English: Let me talk to Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: そそ、そんなムチャな！
English: I-I can't do that!

Japanese: いいかげんにしなさい、 弁護人！
English: Mr. Wright! Would you please get a hold of yourself!?

Japanese: は‥‥はい！
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: 今、審理を終了しますから、 電話なら、そのあとで‥‥。
English: I am about to end today's proceedings. You may take your phone calls after...

Japanese: ま、待ってください！
English: Hold on, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥御剣ッ！
English: Edgeworth! Catch!

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事ッスか！
English: Mr. Edgeworth!?

Japanese: ‥‥おねがいッス！ なんとか、もう少しだけ時間を！
English: Please! You've got to buy us some more time!

Japanese: ‥‥審理中だ。 ‥‥ピッ‥‥
English: Court is in session. ...*beep*...

Japanese: ‥‥シツレイした。
English: I'm sorry, Your Honor. You were saying?

Japanese: 弁護人！ ここは法廷ですぞ！
English: Mr. Wright! This is a court of law!

Japanese: す、すみません！ しかし‥‥！
English: I'm sorry, Your Honor! But...!

Japanese: 私としても不本意です。 しかし‥‥
English: I am reluctant to do this, however...

Japanese: 審理は、明日まで 中断するしかありません！
English: It appears that I have no choice but to suspend proceedings until tomorrow!

Japanese: （もう‥‥ 今度こそダメだ‥‥） (I...
English: This time, I really can't do anything...)

Japanese: それでは、本日の法廷は ここまでとします！
English: Court is now adjourned for the day!

Japanese: ‥‥お待ちいただきたい。
English: Please wait, Your Honor.
(E-Edgeworth!?)

Japanese: な、なんですか、御剣検事。
English: Wh-What is it, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: ３０分、時間をいただきたい。
English: I humbly request another 30 minutes of Your Honor's time.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか？
English: For what purpose?

Japanese: その時間で、この証拠品に 必要な検査をおこなう！
English: We can perform the necessary tests on this piece of evidence in that time!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥しかし、３０分で ケツロンが出るのですか？
English: Hmm... But can you really obtain your results in 30 minutes?

Japanese: 出してごらんに入れる。
English: I believe we can, Your Honor.

Japanese: ここで閉廷して、 １日、時間をとったほうが‥‥。
English: But wouldn't it be better if we adjourned for today and then reconvened tomorrow...?

Japanese: ３０分。 ‥‥おねがいしたい。
English: Thirty minutes. Please, Your Honor. That's all I am asking for.

Japanese: おねがいします！ ‥‥裁判長！
English: Please! Your Honor!

Japanese: しかたがありませんね。
English: Very well.

Japanese: 検察側の要請により、 審理を３０分間、中断します。
English: At the prosecution's request, this court will now take a 30 minute recess.

Japanese: そして、本法廷は、 これ以上の中断は認めません。
English: But be advised that I will not allow another recess today.

Japanese: （た‥‥助かった‥‥のか？）
English: (I'm not sure if this is helping... or hurting us...)

Japanese: それでは、 最後の休憩に入ります！
English: The court will now take its final recess of the day!

Japanese: ３月２３日　午後　２時　４分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: March 23, 2:04 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂！ どうなんだ？　真宵くんのほうは！
English: Wright! Well!? What's going on with Maya's situation!?

Japanese: ‥‥虎狼死家のヤツ‥‥ また、消えちまったらしい。
English: De Killer... It looks like he got away again.

Japanese: ３０分じゃあ‥‥ とても発見はムリだ。
English: Thirty minutes...? We can't find her in that time...

Japanese: う‥‥むむむ‥‥
English: Unnngh...

Japanese: ‥‥状況を報告したまえ！
English: Report!

Japanese: あっ！　その声は、 御剣検事‥‥ッスか！
English: Ah! I-Is that Mr. Edgeworth!?

Japanese: 時間がない。‥‥早く報告を！
English: We don't have time! Just spit it out!

Japanese: は‥‥はッ！
English: R-Right!

Japanese: ヤツらは、ワレワレと すれちがいだったらしいッス。
English: It looks like we just missed them, sir.

Japanese: あわてていたらしく、遺留品が 何点か、残されていたッス。
English: But De Killer left a few things behind by accident in his rush to get away.

Japanese: 遺留品‥‥だと？
English: A few things...?

Japanese: それ‥‥もしかして、 何かの証拠にならないか？
English: Can we use any of them as evidence?

Japanese: ハッハッハッ。‥‥きっと そう言うと思ったッス！
English: Ho ho ho. I thought you'd ask, pal!

Japanese: そう思って、今、遺留品を持って そっちに向かってる途中ッス！
English: I've got the things he left with me right now and I'm on my way over!

Japanese: ホントですか！
English: Really!?

Japanese: ‥‥妙だな。遺留品は、 まず鑑識課が調べるはず‥‥
English: That's odd. Any items like that are usually sent to the crime lab first...

Japanese: そんなの、待ってられないッス。
English: We don't have time to wait for those guys, sir!

Japanese: ヤツらの目を盗んで、 ちょろまかしてきたッス！
English: When those guys weren't looking, I swiped the stuff and ran!

Japanese: え。
English: What!?

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。自分はもう、刑事じゃ ないッスからね‥‥
English: Well... I'm not a detective anymore, so I had to...

Japanese: もう、ホーリツでしばられるのは ゴメンッス！
English: I'm really sorry, sir, but I've got to put the law on hold for now!

Japanese: （そ、それはマズいのでは‥‥）
English: (Sounds bad... I hope he doesn't get in too much trouble over this...)

Japanese: このボロ車でも、あと２０分あれば 裁判所につくッス！
English: With my hunk of junk car, I'd say I'll be there in about 20 minutes, sir!

Japanese: アンシンして 待っててほしいッス！
English: Don't worry! I'll be there! Wait for me!

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥安全運転で 来てくれたまえ。
English: A-Alright... Just get here in one piece.

Japanese: もう、なんぴとたりとも、 自分を止めるコトはできないッス！
English: I'm on a mission and no one can stop me now, sir! No one!

Japanese: ホレホレホレ！ 赤信号なんて、ムシッス！
English: I'm pulling out all the stops and running every red light!

Japanese: （‥‥犯人の遺留品‥‥か）
English: (Items left by the murderer, huh...)

Japanese: （もしかしたら‥‥決定的な 証拠があるかもしれない‥‥！）
English: (Maybe there's something among them that will be decisive enough to end this!)

Japanese: おいッ！　どうした！ イトノコギリ刑事！　応答を‥‥
English: Hey! What's wrong!? Detective Gumshoe! Answer me!

Japanese: なんぴとたりとも‥‥ ‥‥自分を‥‥
English: No one can stop... I'm...
...*beep* *beep* *beep*...

Japanese: な‥‥なんだ？
English: Wh-What happened?

Japanese: ‥‥交通事故だ。 携帯電話もブッこわれたらしい。
English: It sounded like he had an accident. I'm guessing his cell phone broke as well.

Japanese: な‥‥ナニやってんだ あのヒトは‥‥
English: Wh-What was he thinking??

Japanese: とにかく、 早く助けを呼ばないと‥‥！
English: We've got to hurry and call for help!

Japanese: しかし‥‥場所がわからない。
English: But we have no idea where he is.

Japanese: 携帯電話はコワれている。 パトカーではないから無線もない。
English: His cell phone is broken, and he wasn't driving a patrol car, so no radio either.

Japanese: それに‥‥
English: Also...

Japanese: 警察の到着より早く行かなければ、 遺留品も没収されてしまう。
English: If we don't get to those items before they do, the police will take possession of them.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥！
English: No! We can't let that happen!

Japanese: まあ‥‥彼の現在位置を 知る方法でもあればベツだが‥‥
English: Well, if there is a way we can find out where he is, then we stand a chance...

Japanese: （イトノコさん‥‥ミカタなのか、 それとも、最凶のテキなのか？）
English: (Why, oh why did Gumshoe have to get into an accident now!?)

Japanese: （彼の現在位置を、カクジツに 知る方法‥‥か！）
English: (Is there any way to find out exactly where he is at this moment...!?)

Japanese: そうだ‥‥！ １つだけある！　方法が‥‥
English: That's right! There is a way!

Japanese: な、なんだと！
English: What!? How!?

Japanese: イトノコ刑事の現在位置‥‥ コイツが教えてくれるはずだ！
English: I'm sure we can find out where Detective Gumshoe is through this...!

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂。今は裁判ごっこで 遊んでいる場合じゃない。
English: Wright, we're not in the middle of a mock trial here!

Japanese: やっぱりダメか、これじゃあ‥‥
English: I guess this won't work, huh...?

Japanese: しかたない。 私のほうであたってみよう。
English: I guess it's up to me. Alright, I will think of something on my end.

Japanese: 期待はできないが‥‥ 何もしないよりはいい。
English: Don't get your hopes up too high, but I'll try my best.

Japanese: （‥‥ま、マズかったか‥‥？）
English: (...Did I say something wrong...?)

Japanese: ‥‥なぜ、こんなところに 狩魔　冥が‥‥
English: Why are you bringing up Franziska at a time like...

Japanese: あッ！　そうか‥‥
English: Oh, I see!

Japanese: すぐに連絡をしてみよう。 ‥‥望みはうすいが‥‥。
English: I'll try to get in contact with her. The chances are slim, but she's all we have...

Japanese: （あいつ‥‥ 協力してくれるだろうか‥‥？）
English: (Franziska... Will she even want to help us...?)

Japanese: ‥‥ダメだ。そんなの、 わかるワケがない！
English: Argh! It's no use! I can't think of anything!

Japanese: やはり‥‥ムリか‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: わかった。‥‥私のほうで なんとかしてみよう。
English: Alright, I'll try to think of something on my end.

Japanese: 期待はできないが‥‥ 何もしないよりはいい。
English: Don't get your hopes up too high, but I'll try my best.

Japanese: （‥‥ま、マズかったか‥‥？）
English: (...Did I say something wrong...?)
Edgeworth...

Japanese: なんだ。
English: What is it?

Japanese: 人のコト、言えないな ‥‥ぼくも。
English: I don't have any right to judge anyone ever again...

Japanese: 依頼人が有罪なのは、 よく知っている。
English: I know my client is guilty.

Japanese: でも、ぼくが今、 やっていることと言えば‥‥
English: But what I'm doing now...

Japanese: 無実の証人に罪を着せて、 敵の証拠にたよって‥‥
English: I'm pinning the guilt onto someone totally innocent, and using the evidence to do so...

Japanese: 弁護士・成歩堂　龍一も‥‥ 死ぬべきなのかもしれない。
English: It might be my turn to say, "Defense Attorney Phoenix Wright chooses death"...

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂。
English: Wright.

Japanese: くだらん泣きごとに つきあう気はない。
English: It doesn't suit someone like you to cry useless tears.

Japanese: キサマの弁護が正しかったか、 そうでなかったか‥‥
English: Whether you did your job well or not...

Japanese: それがわかるのは、 判決が下された瞬間、だ。
English: That can only be seen after the verdict has been decided.
The verdict...

Japanese: 御剣検事は いらっしゃいますか！
English: Is Prosecutor Edgeworth here!?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ、係官。
English: Yes, bailiff?

Japanese: お電話が入っています。 大至急とのことです。
English: There's a phone call for you, sir. They said it was extremely urgent.

Japanese: ‥‥筆跡の検査が終わったのかも しれない。
English: They're probably finished with the handwriting analysis.

Japanese: ‥‥では、私は行くぞ。
English: I have to go take this call.

Japanese: キサマがするべきは、何か。 ‥‥せいぜい考えることだ。
English: In the mean time, think hard about what it is you must do.

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、王都楼　真悟に この場で、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Matt Engarde...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!
